decks.description: This is a white Cats-and-counters aggro deck that wants to flood the board with small creatures and pump them into lethal attackers. Brimaz, King of Oreskos churns out a 1/1 Cat Soldier token every time it attacks or blocks, immediately giving King of the Pride's 'other Cats get +2/+1' a wide army to boost, and Ingenious Leonin can throw extra +1/+1 counters (plus first strike) onto attacking Cats to push through damage. Basri's Acolyte adds counters to two other creatures on entry, Leonin Vanguard rewards you for having three or more creatures with a combat-trick-sized buff and life gain, and cheap interaction like Pacifism and Impeccable Timing clears blockers or removes attackers so your board can keep swinging. Take Up the Shield and Leonin Scimitar give you ways to protect your key threats mid-combat, making this a low-curve, creature-count-matters aggro deck rather than a spell-heavy control shell.
tips: Attack with Brimaz for free tokens
King of the Pride buffs all Cats
Use Ingenious Leonin on attackers
Pacifism problem blockers
Keep board wide for Leonin Vanguard

decks.description: Giddyap is a lifegain-tribal deck built around Pegasi, Unicorns, and Horses that converts life totals directly into stat growth. Thurid, Mare of Destiny copies every Pegasus/Unicorn/Horse creature spell you cast (getting a free token copy) while pumping all your other Horse-types by +1/+1, and Celestial Unicorn turns any lifegain trigger into a permanent +1/+1 counter, so cards like Shield Mare (gain 3 life on entry or when targeted), Sungrace Pegasus and Sunmane Pegasus (lifelink), and Brightmare (gain life equal to a tapped creature's power) all double as growth engines. Tenacity and Moment of Triumph are lifegain combat tricks that both pump the team and feed Celestial Unicorn, while Outflank scales damage off your creature count and Banishing Light handles problem permanents. The game plan is to stabilize on life early, let Celestial Unicorn and Thurid snowball your board, then swing with a wide, oversized herd.
tips: Cast Horse-types with Thurid out for free copies
Every lifegain trick grows Celestial Unicorn
Use Tenacity to pump and untap
Shield Mare punishes opponent removal
Outflank scales with wide boards

decks.description: This deck is a classic flying Angels tribal shell where Giada, Font of Hope makes every subsequent Angel enter bigger and bigger, since each other Angel gets an additional +1/+1 counter for each Angel you already control - so Serra Angel, Inspiring Overseer, and Youthful Valkyrie all snowball in size the more Angels resolve, and Youthful Valkyrie further grows every time another Angel enters. Faithful Pikemaster smooths draws with scry 2 and gives first-strike protection on your turn, Inspiring Overseer replaces itself with a card and life, and Herald of the Sun and Starnheim Memento can pump flying creatures further. Removal like Destroy Evil, Light of Hope, and Pacifism keeps the skies clear so your evasive Angels can close the game through the air.
tips: Play Angels in sequence for max Giada counters
Youthful Valkyrie grows with each Angel
Scry with Pikemaster to find Angels
Keep skies clear with removal
Race in the air

decks.description: Similar to the other Angels build, this deck leans on Giada, Font of Hope to make each new Angel enter with more +1/+1 counters as your Angel count grows, and pairs that with Herald of War, whose attack trigger adds a +1/+1 counter that also discounts future Angel and Human spells by {1} for each counter on it. Youthful Valkyrie keeps growing off every Angel that enters, Stalwart Valkyrie offers a graveyard-fueled alternate cost to cheat it into play, and Rally of Wings untaps your team while giving flyers a huge +2/+2 swing for a devastating alpha strike. Angelic Edict and Pacifism handle problematic permanents while Faithful Pikemaster smooths draws, making this a tribal flyers deck that gets progressively cheaper and bigger the longer the game goes.
tips: Sequence Angels to maximize Giada counters
Attack with Herald of War for discounts
Cast Stalwart Valkyrie from graveyard fuel
Save Rally of Wings for alpha strike
Use Pacifism on biggest threat

decks.description: This is a Heroic-centric aggro-tempo deck that wants to target its own creatures with cheap pump and card-draw spells to trigger repeated bonuses. Defiant Strike and Moment of Heroism are cantrip/lifegain tricks whose real value is triggering Heroic on creatures like Favored Hoplite (counter plus damage prevention), Akroan Skyguard and Wingsteed Rider (counter and evasion), and Tethmos High Priest, whose Heroic trigger reanimates a cheap creature from your graveyard every time you target it. Brigone, Soldier of Meletis stacks up counters from Heroic triggers that can later be cashed in to draw cards, while Chains of Custody doubles as removal (exiling an opponent's permanent) and protection (giving your creature ward 2). The deck plays out by targeting one or two key Heroic creatures every turn to snowball stats and value rather than spreading buffs across the whole board.
tips: Target the same creature repeatedly
Tethmos High Priest reanimates on Heroic
Bank counters on Brigone to draw
Chains of Custody is removal and protection
Prioritize Favored Hoplite for damage prevention

decks.description: Like the other Heroes build, this deck is built around casting targeted spells on your own creatures to trigger Heroic abilities repeatedly. Ojutai Exemplars offers a menu of Heroic-adjacent value (tap a creature, gain first strike/lifelink, or blink itself) every time you cast a noncreature spell, while Favored Hoplite and Wingsteed Rider get counters and protection from Heroic triggers, and Elite Skirmisher can tap down blockers whenever it's targeted. Repel the Darkness and Defiant Strike are cheap, cantripping tricks that double as removal-adjacent tempo plays and Heroic enablers, Gird for Battle spreads counters across two creatures, and Brigone again banks counters from Heroic triggers to convert into card draw later. Chains of Custody again provides both removal and ward protection, letting you protect your snowballing Heroic threats from opposing interaction.
tips: Target key creatures with cheap tricks
Ojutai Exemplars rewards noncreature spells
Tap blockers with Elite Skirmisher triggers
Brigone counters convert to draws
Protect threats with Chains of Custody

decks.description: This deck is a go-wide token aggro deck where Adeline, Resplendent Cathar's power scales with your total creature count and she creates an attacking token every time you attack, immediately adding to that same count. Raise the Alarm and Ancestral Blade both add extra bodies, Hinterland Sanctifier gains life whenever any of those creatures enters, and Mentor of the Meek lets you draw cards off small creatures entering, turning a wide board into card advantage. Argivian Phalanx and Search Party Captain both get cheaper the more creatures you control or attacked with, rewarding the wide strategy directly, while Dauntless Unity and Make a Stand are team-wide finishers that pump the whole board and can even grant indestructible to survive a wrath. The plan is simply to flood the board, let Adeline and the token generators keep growing your army, and swing for a big alpha strike backed by a combat trick.
tips: Attack with Adeline to spawn extra tokens
Flood board to shrink Phalanx/Captain costs
Mentor of the Meek turns tokens into cards
Make a Stand dodges board wipes
Adeline's power scales with creature count

decks.description: This deck doubles down on the go-wide plan with convoke as a major theme: Venerated Loxodon, Triplicate Spirits, and Conclave Tribunal all get cheaper (or in Loxodon's case, reward) tapping your existing token army, and Venerated Loxodon's entry puts a +1/+1 counter on every creature that helped convoke it, instantly pumping your whole board. Adeline, Resplendent Cathar again scales her power with creature count and mints an attacking token whenever you attack, feeding both convoke costs and Mentor of the Meek's card-draw trigger off small creatures entering. Raise the Alarm, Triplicate Spirits, and Ancestral Blade all add cheap bodies, Hinterland Sanctifier gains life off every creature entering, and Memorial to Glory can sacrifice itself late-game for two more Soldier tokens, keeping the board wide through the whole game to power out big convoke spells and alpha strikes.
tips: Tap tokens to convoke big spells cheaply
Venerated Loxodon pumps convoking creatures
Adeline tokens fuel convoke and Mentor
Memorial to Glory refills late tokens
Swing wide, then finish with tricks

decks.description: This is a lifegain-matters deck where every incidental life gain trigger snowballs into bigger threats and card advantage. Ajani's Pridemate and Qala, Ajani's Pridemate both put a +1/+1 counter on themselves whenever you gain life, and Qala's attack trigger then pumps all your other attackers by that same counter count, so cards like Angel of Mercy (gain 3 on entry), Hinterland Sanctifier (gain 1 whenever a creature enters), Brightmare (gain life equal to a tapped creature's power), Kabira Crossroads, and Faith's Fetters (gain 4 on entry) all directly fuel Qala's growing team-wide buff. Angel of Vitality adds even more value by turning every life gain trigger into life gain plus 1, and buffs itself once you cross 25 life, while Speaker of the Heavens rewards reaching 7+ life above your starting total by minting free 4/4 flying Angel tokens. Revitalize and Desperate Lunge combine card/tempo advantage with more lifegain fuel, making this a value-grindy deck that turns healing into an army of scaling threats.
tips: Every lifegain trigger grows Qala and Pridemate
Attack with Qala to pump the team
Angel of Vitality boosts all lifegain
Chase 25 life for Vitality's buff
Speaker of Heavens mints Angels at high life

decks.description: This deck is another lifegain snowball build, again pairing Ajani's Pridemate and Qala, Ajani's Pridemate to convert every life-gain trigger into growing counters that fuel Qala's team-wide attack buff. Speakeasy Server turns your board width into a burst of life on entry (gaining 1 life per other creature), while Hinterland Sanctifier, Bishop's Soldier, and Brightmare add repeatable or one-shot lifegain to keep feeding those counters, and Angel of Vitality boosts every life gain event by 1 extra and grows itself past 25 life. Valkyrie Harbinger rewards a big lifegain turn directly by creating a free 4/4 flying vigilance Angel token if you gained 4 or more life that turn, giving the deck a clear payoff for stacking multiple lifegain triggers in a single turn (e.g., Bishop's Soldier combat damage plus Brightmare's ETB). Take Up the Shield and Recumbent Bliss round out the deck with protection and removal, while Revitalize provides card advantage alongside more life.
tips: Stack lifegain triggers same turn for Valkyrie Harbinger
Qala and Pridemate grow off any lifegain
Speakeasy Server rewards wide boards
Chase 25 life for Vitality bonus
Recumbent Bliss locks down threats

decks.description: This mono-white lifegain pile is built around turning incidental life gain into permanent stat boosts and game-ending payoffs. Righteous Valkyrie gives you life whenever an Angel or Cleric enters and buffs your whole board +2/+2 once you're 7+ life over your starting total, while Hinterland Sanctifier converts any creature entering into a trickle of life. That life total feeds Ajani's Pridemate and Qala, Ajani's Pridemate, both of which grow with +1/+1 counters every time you gain life, and Qala can then pump your whole attacking team based on her counter count. Angel of Vitality adds one extra life to every gain trigger and grows itself once you cross 25 life, snowballing the whole plan even further. Removal like Banisher Priest and Recumbent Bliss buys time while lifelink creatures and life-gain spells like Moment of Heroism and Deadly Riposte keep pushing your total up, so the deck wins by grinding out incremental life advantages until its creatures are simply bigger than anything the opponent can field.
tips: Gain life to grow Pridemates
Angel of Vitality adds extra life per trigger
Watch for Righteous Valkyrie's +2/+2 threshold
Use Banisher Priest to buy time
Qala pumps attackers off her counters

decks.description: This deck leans hardest into the 'win by gaining absurd amounts of life' angle of the Healers theme, headlined by Felidar Sovereign, which simply wins the game at your upkeep once you hit 40 life, and Rhox Faithmender, which doubles every life gain trigger you generate. With Faithmender in play, cards like Hinterland Sanctifier's 1-life trigger, Revitalize's 3 life, or Faith's Fetters' 4 life all get doubled, letting you race to Felidar Sovereign's 40-life threshold shockingly fast. Ajani's Pridemate and Qala, Ajani's Pridemate again turn each of those doubled life gains into permanent +1/+1 counters, and Angel of Vitality adds another layer, adding +1 to every gain and growing itself past 25 life. The overall plan is to stall with removal and lifelink bodies like Mesa Unicorn while your life total balloons, closing the game either through a giant Qala-led alpha strike or simply by triggering Felidar Sovereign's win condition.
tips: Prioritize casting Rhox Faithmender early
Doubled life gain accelerates Felidar Sovereign win
Stack Faithmender before life-gain spells
Use removal to stall until 40 life
Pridemates snowball off doubled triggers

decks.description: A go-wide +1/+1 counter deck that wants to spread counters across the board and then have a handful of payoff creatures multiply them further. Gavony Silversmith and Sandsteppe Outcast seed early counters, and Patron of the Valiant then puts an additional counter on every creature that already has one, turning a modest spread into a much bigger board state in one swing. Generous Pup takes this further by triggering off any counters being placed to distribute a counter to every other creature once per turn, so playing Patron of the Valiant or Gavony Silversmith after Generous Pup is online spreads counters to your whole team at once. Urdnan, Dromoka Warrior rewards all this counter density by granting first strike (or double strike, with two or more counters) to an attacker carrying counters, while Ajani, Adversary of Tyrants both keeps adding counters and can reanimate small creatures from the graveyard. The deck plays a normal curve of white bodies backed by Banishing Light for removal and Swift Response to punish tapped blockers, aiming to overwhelm with a wide, counter-laden team that hits exceptionally hard once double strike kicks in.
tips: Land Generous Pup before counter spells
Patron of the Valiant doubles existing counters
Urdnan turns counters into double strike
Sequence Ajani's -2 to reanimate cheap bodies
Use Banishing Light on biggest threat

decks.description: This counters deck focuses on spreading +1/+1 counters efficiently and turning creature deaths into more value rather than just going wide immediately. Basri's Acolyte and Sandsteppe Outcast distribute early counters, and Patron of the Valiant then adds another counter to every creature that already carries one, rewarding you for spreading counters thin first. Basri's Lieutenant and Steadfast Sentry both convert deaths into more counters or tokens — Basri's Lieutenant creates a 2/2 vigilant Knight token whenever a counter-bearing creature you control dies, while Steadfast Sentry puts a counter on another creature when it dies, meaning combat trades or removal aimed at your board often nets you extra value instead of just losses. Urdnan, Dromoka Warrior again rewards counter density with first or double strike on attack, and Basri's Solidarity acts as a mass pump that also sets up Basri's Lieutenant tokens if your creatures start dying afterward. Cave of Temptation gives a late-game mana sink to push extra counters onto a key threat, rounding out a resilient, attrition-friendly counters strategy.
tips: Spread counters before Patron of the Valiant
Basri's Lieutenant turns deaths into tokens
Attack with counter creatures for Urdnan bonus
Sac Cave of Temptation late for counters
Trade creatures fearlessly with death triggers up

decks.description: This is a support-focused +1/+1 counters deck that spreads counters wide through repeatable triggers rather than relying on a single big payoff. Expedition Raptor, Relief Captain, and Jubilant Mascot all use support to place counters on multiple other creatures as they enter or each combat, while Shoulder to Shoulder replicates the effect as an instant-speed spell that also draws a card. Generous Pup ties this together by triggering off any counters being placed to spread an additional counter to every other creature once per turn, so casting a support creature after Generous Pup lands effectively counters your whole team at once. Urdnan, Dromoka Warrior rewards the resulting counter density by granting first strike or double strike to an attacking counter-carrier, and Steadfast Unicorn's activated pump plus Take Up the Shield's indestructible clause give you ways to protect your board or push through extra damage in the late game. Overall this deck wants to steadily build a wide, evenly-buffed team and then swing with Urdnan's combat bonus doing the heavy lifting.
tips: Play Generous Pup before support triggers
Support spreads counters cheaply
Urdnan grants double strike with 2+ counters
Shoulder to Shoulder replaces itself
Use Take Up the Shield to save creatures

decks.description: This deck is built around Mikaeus, the Lunarch as a repeatable counter engine: he enters with counters equal to X and can tap to remove one of his own counters and spread a counter to every other creature you control, letting you buff your whole team turn after turn without spending cards. That synergizes directly with the deck's mentor creatures — Light of the Legion, Parhelion Patrol, and Sunhome Stalwart all put counters on weaker attackers when they attack, and Urdnan, Dromoka Warrior grants first strike or double strike to any attacking creature that already carries counters, so a board pre-loaded by Mikaeus turns mentor triggers and Urdnan's bonus into a much bigger combat swing. Gavony Silversmith and Gird for Battle add more up-front counter distribution, while Light of the Legion's death trigger gives every white creature a counter as a parting gift. The deck plays a steady curve of efficient white bodies backed by Banishing Light and Swift Response for removal, aiming to grind out an ever-growing, first/double-striking army.
tips: Activate Mikaeus every turn possible
Mentor targets weaker attackers first
Urdnan rewards counter-heavy attackers
Light of the Legion buffs team on death
Sequence Gavony Silversmith before attacking

decks.description: A white equipment aggro deck that wants to get cheap bodies onto the board and immediately dress them up with gear for evasive, damage-doubling attacks. Danitha Capashen, Paragon comes down with first strike, vigilance, and lifelink already attached, and also discounts every Aura and Equipment spell you cast, making it easier to chain gear onto her or onto Ancestral Blade's and Valkyrie's Sword's and Mirran Bardiche's free token bodies. Valkyrie's Sword and Mirran Bardiche both create their own creature (a 4/4 flying vigilance Angel and a 2/2 Rebel respectively) and immediately attach for free, letting you deploy a threat and equipment in a single card. Sunspear Shikari only needs to be equipped with anything to gain first strike and lifelink, making even the cheapest Ancestral Blade attachment a big upgrade. Mace of the Valiant scales up over time as more creatures enter, growing into a huge vigilance boost. The deck closes games by stacking equipment onto a small number of resilient, evasive threats while using Divine Arrow and Pacifism to clear blockers out of the way.
tips: Play Danitha early to discount gear
Equip Sunspear Shikari for lifelink
Mirran Bardiche and Valkyrie's Sword self-attach
Mace of the Valiant grows over time
Clear blockers with Divine Arrow/Pacifism

decks.description: This equipment deck cares about equipment quantity itself as a stat, not just individual bonuses. Militant Inquisitor gets bigger for every Equipment you control, and Stone Haven Outfitter gives all equipped creatures +1/+1 while also drawing you a card whenever an equipped creature dies, turning combat losses into card advantage. Plate Armor's equip cost drops by {1} for every other Equipment you control, so once you've assembled a few cheap equipments like Ancestral Blade or Hexgold Hoverwings, Plate Armor can come down for as little as one or two mana and slap ward and a huge +3/+3 onto a creature. Hexgold Hoverwings and Mirran Bardiche both create their own attached token bodies via 'For Mirrodin!', letting you build a board of equipped threats quickly, while Danitha Capashen, Paragon discounts all your Aura/Equipment spells to accelerate the whole plan. Light the Way offers flexible utility, either protecting a key equipped creature by bouncing it or growing an attacker with a counter and untap, making the deck resilient as well as aggressive.
tips: Stack Equipment count for Militant Inquisitor
Plate Armor gets cheaper per Equipment
Hexgold Hoverwings/Mirran Bardiche self-attach
Stone Haven Outfitter draws off deaths
Danitha discounts your Equipment spells

decks.description: This equipment deck blends aggressive equip-and-swing plays with artifact/enchantment recursion. Militant Inquisitor scales with your Equipment count while Flutterfox gains flying automatically as soon as you control any artifact or enchantment, meaning equipping it or even just playing Pacifism turns it into an evasive threat. Trusty Retriever gives flexible value, either entering with a counter or returning an artifact/enchantment like a fallen Equipment from the graveyard back to hand, so losing a key equipment to removal isn't as painful. Maul of the Skyclaves auto-attaches on entry, immediately granting +2/+2, flying, and first strike, making it one of the most efficient threats in the deck, while Mirran Bardiche and Valkyrie's Sword generate their own attached bodies for free. Danitha Capashen, Paragon again discounts the Aura and Equipment spells that make up the bulk of the deck, and Resistance Reunited protects your equipped creatures with indestructible during combat, letting you attack profitably into removal or blocks.
tips: Maul of the Skyclaves auto-attaches for tempo
Flutterfox flies off any artifact/enchantment
Trusty Retriever rebuys lost Equipment
Danitha discounts your gear
Use Resistance Reunited to push through blocks

decks.description: This equipment deck leans on cheap, efficient gear and a couple of standout combos to close games. Thopter Architect grants flying to any target creature whenever an artifact you control enters, so playing Ancestral Blade (which creates its own 1/1 Soldier token) or any other equipment triggers a temporary evasion buff, letting even ground-bound bodies punch through in the air. Sunspear Shikari and Militant Inquisitor both reward simply having equipment attached or in play, gaining first strike/lifelink or scaling power respectively, while Danitha Capashen, Paragon discounts your Aura and Equipment spells to help you deploy gear faster than usual. Godsend stands out as a powerful removal-attached-to-a-threat piece: it pumps its bearer +3/+3 and lets you exile a blocking or blocked creature, permanently shutting off future copies of that card by name. The deck rounds out with Pacifism and Deadly Riposte for interaction, aiming to build one or two heavily-equipped, evasive threats that are hard for the opponent to profitably block or answer.
tips: Trigger Thopter Architect with any artifact
Godsend removes blockers permanently
Equip Sunspear Shikari for lifelink
Danitha discounts equipment spells
Stack equipment on one evasive threat

decks.description: This mono-white deck wants to flood the board with enchantments and Auras to trigger Constellation abilities over and over. Eidolon of Astral Winds and Captivating Unicorn and Favored of Iroas all read 'whenever an enchantment you control enters', so every Aura you cast (Chosen by Heliod, Dreadful Apathy, Indomitable Will) or enchantment permanent (Banishing Light) restarts the chain: pumping a creature to 4/4 flying, tapping down a blocker, and granting double strike all at once. Psemilla, Meletian Poet also churns out a 2/2 Nymph token the first time you cast an enchantment spell each turn and turns into a huge lifelinker once you have five-plus enchantments out, so the deck naturally builds toward a wide, evasive board that closes games fast once Constellation triggers stack up. Archon of Falling Stars adds resilience by rebuying an enchantment from the graveyard when it dies, letting you re-trigger Constellation a second time.
tips: Sequence Auras to chain Constellation triggers
Cast an enchantment before combat for Eidolon pump
Use Captivating Unicorn to tap down blockers
Rebuy Auras with Archon of Falling Stars
Race with Psemilla's Nymph tokens

decks.description: A slightly more lifegain-leaning version of the mono-white enchantments plan, still anchored by Psemilla, Meletian Poet making a 2/2 Nymph token off your first enchantment spell each turn and swinging as a lifelinking 6/6 once you hit five enchantments. Eidolon of Astral Winds keeps the Constellation pump train running on any enchantment entering, while Cartouche of Solidarity doubles as a combat trick and a token generator, making a 1/1 Warrior and buffing a creature with first strike the moment it resolves. The life-gain package (Golden-Tail Disciple, Nyx-Fleece Ram, Sunblade Samurai's Channel) grinds out incidental life to stay ahead of races, and cheap removal Auras like Cage of Hands and Banishing Light lock down or exile problem permanents while still counting as enchantments for your other triggers.
tips: Cast enchantments early for Constellation value
Bounce Cage of Hands to reuse removal
Cartouche of Solidarity for tokens plus first strike
Grind life totals with incidental gain
Swing wide once Psemilla flips on

decks.description: This build turns lifegain into raw stats via Nykthos Paragon, which puts a +1/+1 counter on every creature you control equal to however much life you gained that turn, once per turn. Divine Favor's 'gain 3 life' trigger and Nyx-Fleece Ram's upkeep life gain feed that engine directly, and once Psemilla, Meletian Poet flips on with five-plus enchantments and gains lifelink, her combat damage itself can trigger Nykthos Paragon for another wave of counters. Starfield Mystic discounts your enchantment spells and grows every time one of your enchantments dies, giving you a second payoff for the same Aura-heavy shell. The overall plan is to cast enchantments cheaply, gain incidental life, and watch your whole board grow permanently rather than just for a turn, closing games with a team of counter-laden creatures.
tips: Gain life before Nykthos Paragon trigger
Chain enchantments to discount with Starfield Mystic
Let Psemilla's lifelink feed counters
Protect Nykthos Paragon at all costs
Stack Auras for permanent stat growth

decks.description: This version leans into flying tokens and evasive damage. Archon of Sun's Grace creates a 2/2 flying Pegasus every time an enchantment enters and gives all your Pegasi lifelink, so casting Banishing Light, Angelic Gift, or Dreadful Apathy each spins up another evasive lifelinking attacker. Favored of Iroas piggybacks on the same Constellation triggers to grant itself double strike, and Psemilla, Meletian Poet keeps adding Nymph tokens and eventually swings as a lifelinking beater once you hit five enchantments. Angelic Gift and Spirited Companion replace themselves with card draw, keeping your hand full of enchantments to keep the Constellation engine fed, while Archon of Falling Stars lets you rebuy a key enchantment from the graveyard for another round of triggers.
tips: Cast enchantments for Pegasus tokens
Stack triggers with Favored of Iroas
Refill hand with cantrip Auras
Swarm skies with lifelinking Pegasi
Rebuy key enchantments after they die

decks.description: This mono-blue deck is built around cheap evasive creatures and instant/sorcery payoffs. Murmuring Mystic converts every instant or sorcery you cast into a 1/1 flying Bird Illusion token, while Haunting Figment becomes unblockable the turn you've cast a spell, rewarding you for jamming cheap cantrips like Opt and Phantasmal Form. Oneirophage grows a +1/+1 counter every time you draw a card, so Opt, Phantasmal Form, and Supreme Will's card-selection mode all directly feed it bigger and bigger. Krovikan Mist scales off your total Illusion count, and Lord of the Unreal blankets your Illusions with hexproof, protecting your token army and key threats from removal. Pol Jamaar, Illusionist can refill your hand by naming a creature type your board is full of, capping off a deck that wants to bounce, counter, and draw its way to overwhelming card advantage.
tips: Grow Oneirophage with card draw
Cast spells to fuel Murmuring Mystic tokens
Attack unblocked with Haunting Figment
Protect the board with Lord of the Unreal
Name a creature type for Pol Jamaar draw

decks.description: This Ninjutsu deck wants to attack with a cheap unblocked creature, then swap it for a Ninja to sneak in extra value. Ninja of the Deep Hours, Moon-Circuit Hacker, and Prosperous Thief all have Ninjutsu, letting you return an unblocked attacker to hand and put them into play tapped and attacking instead, triggering their combat-damage abilities (draw a card, make a Treasure) without ever being blocked. Taeko, the Patient Avalanche grows whenever a creature leaves the battlefield without dying (exactly what Ninjutsu does) and can make an attacker unblockable outright, smoothing your Ninjutsu switches even further. Prosperous Thief turns any Ninja or Rogue combat damage into a Treasure token, fueling your curve, while Phantom Ninja is simply always unblockable to guarantee a Ninjutsu target every turn.
tips: Attack with cheap creature, swap in via Ninjutsu
Use Phantom Ninja as reliable Ninjutsu enabler
Grow Taeko off Ninjutsu returns
Chain Treasure from Prosperous Thief
Bounce blockers with Fading Hope or Moonsnare Specialist

decks.description: A defensive-toughness value deck built around Plagon, Lord of the Beach, whose ability lets any creature you control deal damage equal to toughness instead of power, turning your wall of high-toughness blockers (Shorecomber Crab, Purple-Crystal Crab, Tidepool Turtle) into real attackers when needed. Plagon's enters-the-battlefield trigger draws a card for every creature with toughness greater than power, so the deck's crab-heavy, defensive-statted creatures directly translate into card advantage the moment Plagon lands. Kapsho Kitefins taps down opposing creatures every time it or another creature enters, combining with bounce-and-replay effects like Yarok's Wavecrasher (bounce your own creature to reuse its enter trigger) to repeatedly lock down the opponent's board. Scry and card selection (Sigiled Starfish, Tidepool Turtle, Omen of the Sea, Delightful Discovery) smooth draws while you wait to grind out an advantage with tempo plays like Run Ashore and Starlight Snare.
tips: Land Plagon for a card draw burst
Use Plagon's ability to attack with toughness
Bounce Kapsho Kitefins for repeat taps
Lean on scry to smooth draws
Lock down threats with Starlight Snare

decks.description: Similar to its counterpart, this deck centers on Plagon, Lord of the Beach converting a bench of high-toughness creatures (Shorecomber Crab, Tidepool Turtle, Sigiled Starfish) into a burst of card draw on entry and letting them deal damage based on toughness instead of power when attacking. Gilded Scuttler is unblockable and stuns down a blocker as it enters, giving you a reliable evasive clock while your value creatures dig for answers. Shipwreck Dowser rebuys spent instants and sorceries like Delightful Discovery or Opt, and its Prowess means it grows whenever you cast those noncreature spells, giving the deck a second angle of pressure. The overall gameplan is patient: scry and cantrip with Opt, Delightful Discovery, and the scry-lands/creatures to find Plagon or removal, then close out with evasive or stunned-down attackers.
tips: Deploy toughness creatures before Plagon
Rebuy spells with Shipwreck Dowser
Push through with unblockable Gilded Scuttler
Scry aggressively to hit land drops
Use Plagon's ability to swing with toughness

decks.description: This mill/self-mill deck powers a graveyard engine anchored by Neerdiv, Devious Diver, who mills opponents whenever it taps and draws you a card plus a +1/+1 counter whenever you cast a spell from your graveyard or activate a graveyard ability. Flashback spells like Deep Analysis and Silent Departure, along with graveyard-activated abilities on Expendable Lackey and Merfolk Pupil, are exactly the triggers that grow Neerdiv and refill your hand simultaneously. Organ Hoarder and Wall of Lost Thoughts self-mill to stock the graveyard with fuel, while Stitched Drake gets cast for cheap by exiling a creature card from that same graveyard as an evasive flier. Sudden Insight scales with the variety of mana values already binned in your yard, rewarding the self-mill plan with a potentially huge card-draw spell late game.
tips: Mill yourself to fuel graveyard plays
Flashback spells to grow Neerdiv
Cheat out Stitched Drake via exile cost
Time Sudden Insight for max value
Use Neerdiv taps to mill opponent

decks.description: This build doubles down on milling the opponent as a real win condition while still supporting the Neerdiv, Devious Diver graveyard-value engine. Screaming Swarm and Towering-Wave Mystic mill the opponent whenever you attack or deal damage, and Neerdiv itself mills equal to its power whenever it becomes tapped, so simply attacking with your team chips away at their library. Waker of Waves shrinks opposing attackers and can discard itself to dig two cards deep, feeding the graveyard for later value. Cackling Counterpart's Flashback lets you copy a creature twice from the same card, and Deep Analysis's Flashback plus graveyard-fueled Sudden Insight give you sustained card advantage once your yard is stocked from Wall of Lost Thoughts and Draugr Thought-Thief's mill effects. The deck plays a patient, grindy game, milling the opponent while drawing extra cards off its own graveyard density.
tips: Attack to mill via Screaming Swarm
Grow Neerdiv with graveyard casts
Flashback Cackling Counterpart for a second copy
Stock graveyard early with mill creatures
Save Deep Analysis flashback for late game

decks.description: This deck is a mono-blue Wizards value pile built around ETB (enters-the-battlefield) triggers that bounce creatures, draw cards, or dig through the library, all amplified by Naban, Dean of Iteration. Naban doubles up ETB triggers from Wizards, so creatures like Academy Journeymage (bounce a creature) and Exclusion Mage (bounce a creature) become two-for-one tempo swings when Naban is out, and Archaeomancer's return-an-instant-or-sorcery trigger can rebuy Remand or Concentrate twice over. Cheap card selection from Faerie Seer's scry 2, Augur of Bolas' instant/sorcery dig, and Cloudkin Seer's cantrip keeps the hand full while Academy Journeymage gets a discount off other Wizards on the battlefield. The overall plan is to grind opponents out with repeated bounce effects and card advantage rather than racing on the ground, using Choking Tethers and Starlight Snare to buy time against aggressive boards.
tips: Play Naban before other Wizards ETBs
Chain bounce creatures for tempo
Archaeomancer rebuys Remand/Concentrate
Use cycling spells to smooth draws
Hold up Starlight Snare on threats

decks.description: A tighter mono-blue Wizards tempo-control shell that leans even harder on repeatable bounce and tap effects to lock opponents out of attacking or blocking effectively. Naban, Dean of Iteration again doubles ETB triggers from Wizards, turning Frost Trickster's tap-and-don't-untap effect or Academy Journeymage's bounce into devastating two-for-one plays, and it can double Aether Channeler's modal choice for two bounces, two cards, or two tokens in one entry. Expedition Diviner turns into a card-draw engine whenever it dies as long as another Wizard is present, rewarding a board full of Wizards. Shipwreck Dowser rebuys instants like Remand, and cheap cantrips (Augur of Bolas, Faerie Seer) keep the deck flowing toward its bounce/tempo plan while Starlight Snare and Chilling Trap lock down bigger threats.
tips: Sequence Naban before ETB Wizards
Double Aether Channeler with Naban
Keep board full of Wizards for Expedition Diviner
Shipwreck Dowser rebuys Remand
Tap down attackers with Frost Trickster

decks.description: A mono-blue flyers tribal deck that wants to fill the skies and turn every attack into a card-draw event. Cynette, Jelly Drover pumps all flyers +1/+1 and makes a flying Jellyfish token whenever it enters or dies, while Windreader Sphinx lets you draw a card any time a flyer attacks (yours or an opponent's), and Tide Skimmer specifically draws when you attack with two or more flyers. Waterkin Shaman gets bigger every time a flyer enters, and Warden of Evos Isle discounts your flying creature spells to help you flood the board faster. Aerial Guide grants flying to a non-flying attacker, letting Serpentine Ambush turn a ground creature into a 5/5 attacker that then also benefits from Aerial Guide's flying grant. The deck's card draw suite (Winged Words, Gust of Wind) gets cheaper the more flyers you control, so the game plan is simply to curve out flyers, keep attacking in the air, and let the card draw triggers snowball.
tips: Curve flyers to trigger Waterkin Shaman
Attack with 2+ flyers for Tide Skimmer
Aerial Guide + Serpentine Ambush combo
Cynette pumps team on ETB and death
Use Warden of Evos Isle to flood board

decks.description: This flyers deck blends the classic aerial tribal shell with extra card selection and a reanimation-style loop on its flagship threat. Cavalier of Gales draws three and rebuys two cards to the top of the library on entry, and when it dies it shuffles back into the library and scries 2, meaning you can find and recast it again later for repeated value. Cynette, Jelly Drover buffs flyers and spawns a Jellyfish token on entry or death, stacking with Warden of Evos Isle's cost reduction on flying creature spells to help you rebuild a board of evasive threats quickly. Cartouche of Knowledge draws a card and gives a creature flying, and pairs with Trial of Knowledge, which bounces itself back to hand whenever a Cartouche you control enters, letting you replay Trial of Knowledge's draw-three-discard-one effect over and over for card advantage. Majestic Metamorphosis can turn any creature (or Cynette's Jellyfish token) into a 4/4 flying Angel with a cantrip attached, giving the deck a way to punch through stalled boards.
tips: Cartouche of Knowledge loops Trial of Knowledge
Cavalier of Gales recurs itself via death trigger
Buff a token with Majestic Metamorphosis
Use Warden of Evos Isle for tempo
Cynette's tokens fuel go-wide attacks

decks.description: A flyers tribal deck anchored by the planeswalker Mu Yanling, Sky Dancer, whose -3 ability creates a 4/4 flying Elemental Bird token and whose +2 shuts down an opposing attacker by stripping flying and reducing power. Cynette, Jelly Drover and Windstorm Drake both pump the team's flyers, while Tide Skimmer rewards attacking with two or more flyers with a card draw, and Thopter Mechanic grows with a +1/+1 counter whenever you draw your second card in a turn (easily enabled by Tide Skimmer, Winged Words, or Cloudkin Seer) and leaves behind a flying Thopter token when it dies. Aerial Guide can hand flying to a ground creature, which combines with Serpentine Ambush to turn any creature into a temporary 5/5 flying attacker. The deck aims to protect Mu Yanling early with Starlight Snare or tempo plays, then grind toward -8 for the card-drawing Island emblem while its flying squad closes the game.
tips: Protect Mu Yanling with removal
Tide Skimmer/Winged Words trigger Thopter Mechanic
Aerial Guide + Serpentine Ambush for a surprise 5/5 flyer
Ult Mu Yanling for card advantage
Use -3 for a 4/4 flying blocker/attacker

decks.description: This flyers deck focuses on pumping a wide evasive board and converting extra flying creatures into direct damage and card draw. Sprite Noble buffs other flyers' toughness passively and can tap to give them all +1/+0 for a big alpha strike, while Cynette, Jelly Drover adds a flat +1/+1 to flyers and creates a Jellyfish token on entry or death. Tide Skimmer draws a card whenever you attack with two or more flyers, rewarding the deck's go-wide air plan, and Aerial Guide can grant flying to a ground creature to help hit that two-flyer threshold. Stolen by the Fae is the standout tempo/value combo piece: it bounces an opposing creature and creates that many 1/1 flying Faerie tokens equal to its mana value, instantly refilling your board with more flying attackers after removing a blocker. Majestic Metamorphosis can also turn any permanent, including a Faerie token, into a 4/4 flying Angel for a surprise finisher.
tips: Stolen by the Fae bounces and makes flyers
Tap Sprite Noble before attacking
Aerial Guide enables Tide Skimmer's two-flyer draw
Turn a token into 4/4 Angel with Metamorphosis
Cynette buffs whole flying squad

decks.description: A mono-blue flash tempo deck designed to hold up mana and play its whole hand on the opponent's turn, ambushing them with creatures and removal at instant speed. Thryx, the Sudden Storm has flash itself and discounts and protects your biggest spells (mana value 5+) from countermagic, while Brineborn Cutthroat grows a +1/+1 counter every time you cast a spell on an opponent's turn, rewarding the deck's entire flash-heavy gameplan. Stinging Lionfish lets you tap or untap a permanent the first time you flash something in on an opponent's turn, which can tap down a blocker before an attack or untap a land for extra mana. Chrome Prowler taps down a blocker on entry, and Capture Sphere/Slimebind lock down or cripple threats, all castable at instant speed to keep mana open. Delightful Discovery gets progressively cheaper the more spells opponents cast, turning their own tempo plays into a cheap card-draw spell for you.
tips: Hold mana, play everything at flash speed
Brineborn Cutthroat grows off-turn spells
Stinging Lionfish taps blockers pre-combat
Delightful Discovery cheaper vs spell-heavy foes
Chrome Prowler taps down attackers

decks.description: This flash-tempo deck doubles down on ambush interaction, using cheap instant-speed threats to counter, tap, and disrupt opponents right when they least expect it. Voracious Greatshark flashes in to counter an artifact or creature spell outright, functioning as both a body and a Counterspell in one card, while Pestermite and Stinging Lionfish both offer tap/untap effects on entry or spell-cast that can stop a blocker or attacker cold. Naiad of Hidden Coves discounts all your spells on opponents' turns, synergizing with Brineborn Cutthroat, which grows a +1/+1 counter every time you cast something during an opponent's turn — the cheaper spells from Naiad let you trigger Cutthroat's growth more often within a turn. Delightful Discovery becomes nearly free the more the opponent casts spells, rewarding a reactive, patient playstyle where you hold up mana and let your opponent walk into countermagic and cheap removal like Capture Sphere and Starlit Mantle.
tips: Naiad discounts spells for more Cutthroat triggers
Voracious Greatshark counters key threats
Tap blockers with Pestermite/Stinging Lionfish
Hold mana for opponent's turn
Starlit Mantle protects your key creature

decks.description: A flash-heavy tempo-control deck built to disrupt the opponent's plans at instant speed while quietly building a board. Venser, Shaper Savant can bounce any spell or permanent on entry, giving you a pseudo-counterspell or removal that also leaves behind a 3/3 flash body, and Naiad of Hidden Coves reduces the cost of everything you cast on opponents' turns, which directly fuels Brineborn Cutthroat's ability to grow a +1/+1 counter for every spell cast during an opponent's turn. Pestermite and Breaching Hippocamp both offer flexible tap/untap effects on entry — Breaching Hippocamp can untap another creature you control, refunding mana or resetting a tapped attacker/blocker. Stinging Lionfish adds another layer of tap/untap disruption the first time you cast a spell each opponent's turn. Supreme Will offers modal flexibility between a soft counter and card selection, while Starlight Snare and Starlit Mantle round out the deck's mix of lockdown and protection, all playable at instant speed to keep opponents guessing.
tips: Venser bounces problem spells/permanents
Naiad discounts spells for extra Cutthroat counters
Breaching Hippocamp resets tapped creatures
Hold up Supreme Will for flexibility
Play reactively on opponent's turn

decks.description: This flash tempo deck is built to punish opponents for tapping out, using a suite of instant-speed creatures and cheap interaction to swing tempo on their turn. Venser, Shaper Savant bounces a spell or permanent for a strong tempo swing attached to a body, while Wavebreak Hippocamp and Stinging Lionfish both reward casting your first spell on an opponent's turn — Wavebreak Hippocamp draws you a card and Stinging Lionfish lets you tap or untap a permanent, meaning a single flash spell on their turn can both net you a card and disrupt their board. Brineborn Cutthroat continues to grow with every spell cast on an opponent's turn, making it a natural payoff for this deck's reactive plan. Fading Hope bounces a creature and scries if it's cheap, Soul Read offers a soft counter or card draw, and Uncomfortable Chill can both weaken blockers and cantrip, all fitting the deck's instant-speed, hold-up-mana identity.
tips: Cast first spell early on opponent's turn
Wavebreak Hippocamp + Stinging Lionfish combo value
Brineborn Cutthroat rewards off-turn spells
Venser bounces problem permanents
Hold mana, ambush at instant speed

decks.description: This mono-blue deck is built around drawing multiple cards per turn to trigger a suite of 'second card' payoffs while Syr Elenora, the Discerning grows into a genuine threat off a stuffed hand. Reckless Scholar, Sky-Eel School, and Cartouche of Knowledge's card draw combine with cheap cantrips like Blink of an Eye to consistently hit a second draw each turn, which pumps Faerie Vandal and Lat-Nam Adept with +1/+1 counters and grants Mystic Skyfish evasive flying. Overflowing Insight is the payoff blowout, handing you seven cards in one shot to refuel Syr Elenora's power and keep the second-draw engine running for several turns straight, while Starlight Snare locks down a blocker or attacker so your card-advantage engine can close out the game unmolested.
tips: Chain two draws each turn
Cast Overflowing Insight to refill
Grow Syr Elenora with a full hand
Lock threats with Starlight Snare
Use cheap cantrips to trigger counters

decks.description: A mono-blue draw-two engine deck that leans even harder into the 'second card drawn' mechanic with Teferi's Ageless Insight doubling every draw past your first each turn, making it trivial to trigger Faerie Vandal and Mystic Skyfish's abilities multiple times. Cheap cantrips like Drag Under and Uncomfortable Chill smooth the curve while bouncing or shrinking opposing attackers, and sacrifice-for-cards outlets like Witching Well and Memorial to Genius keep the hand full to feed Syr Elenora, the Discerning's power. Phantasmal Shieldback punishes removal or targeted disruption by drawing a card the moment it's targeted, turning opposing interaction into more fuel for the deck's grindy card-advantage plan.
tips: Play Teferi's Ageless Insight early
Double up draws for value
Bait removal with Phantasmal Shieldback
Bounce blockers with Drag Under
Keep hand full for Syr Elenora

decks.description: This mono-blue deck centers on the same second-card-draw synergy package as its siblings, powered by Sphinx of Enlightenment's huge card-advantage swing that hands you three cards off a single flying body. Reckless Scholar and Sky-Eel School provide repeatable or one-shot loot effects to hit that second draw, feeding +1/+1 counters onto Lat-Nam Adept and Thopter Mechanic (which also leaves behind a flying Thopter when it dies), while Mystic Skyfish borrows evasion for a turn. Cartouche of Knowledge and Trial of Knowledge form a small loop where each Cartouche cast returns Trial of Knowledge to hand, letting you recast it for another three-card draw-and-discard, refilling the hand to inflate Syr Elenora, the Discerning's power.
tips: Recur Trial of Knowledge with Cartouches
Draw two to trigger counters
Land Sphinx of Enlightenment for value
Snare problem creatures with Starlight Snare
Keep casting cantrips to grow Elenora

decks.description: A more controlling mono-blue build that pairs the second-draw counter package with two Kraken payoffs that trigger off any card draw at all, not just the second one. Nadir Kraken grows a +1/+1 counter and spawns a 1/1 Tentacle token each time you pay {1} after drawing, while Tolarian Kraken lets you tap down blockers or untap your own creatures every time you draw and pay {1}, giving this deck a tempo/board-control angle its siblings lack. Cheap card selection like Opt and Soul Read (which can counter a spell or just cantrip) smooths draws, Drag Under bounces threats while replacing itself, and Phantasmal Shieldback again punishes removal by drawing a card on death, all while Syr Elenora, the Discerning scales with the resulting full hand.
tips: Pay {1} off every draw for Krakens
Tap opposing blockers with Tolarian Kraken
Bait removal with Phantasmal Shieldback
Hold Soul Read as flexible counter
Grow hand size for Elenora's power

decks.description: A mono-blue artifacts deck that goes wide with cheap Thopter tokens and scales them up with anthem effects. Sai, Master Thopterist spits out a 1/1 flying Thopter every time you cast an artifact spell, and with Experimental Aviator and Whirlermaker also generating Thopters, Chief of the Foundry's +1/+1 boost to artifact creatures quickly turns a swarm of 1/1 fliers into a real clock. Filigree Attendant's power scales directly with your artifact count, so it grows automatically as the Thopter army builds, while Gearseeker Serpent gets cheaper the more artifacts you control thanks to affinity, letting it come down early as an unblockable finisher later in the game.
tips: Cast artifacts to trigger Sai
Buff the swarm with Chief of the Foundry
Watch Filigree Attendant scale with artifacts
Use affinity to cheat out Gearseeker Serpent
Stun key blockers with Gilded Scuttler

decks.description: This mono-blue artifacts deck builds the same Thopter-token core as its counterpart but shifts toward a more resilient, grindy shell. Sai, Master Thopterist and Experimental Aviator flood the board with flying tokens while Chief of the Foundry pumps them all, and Shimmer Dragon becomes effectively unkillable once you hit four artifacts, since it gains hexproof and can also tap two artifacts to draw a card, turning your token army into a card-advantage engine. Malfunction locks down a problematic permanent, Treasure Keeper offers a free spell off the top of your library when it dies, and Fireshrieker turns any evasive creature into a double-striking finisher, closing games quickly once the board stabilizes.
tips: Hit four artifacts for hexproof Dragon
Tap artifacts for cards with Shimmer Dragon
Equip Fireshrieker for double strike kills
Lock permanents with Malfunction
Value trade with Treasure Keeper on death

decks.description: An affinity-driven mono-blue artifacts deck that wants to flood the battlefield with cheap artifacts to discount its payoffs and draw extra cards. Thought Monitor, Myr Enforcer, and Thoughtcast all get cheaper for every artifact you control, letting you deploy big bodies or draw two cards for very little mana once the board fills up, and Padeem, Consul of Innovation both protects your artifacts with hexproof and refills your hand every upkeep as long as you control the biggest artifact on the board. Patchwork Automaton grows a permanent +1/+1 counter every time you cast an artifact spell, rewarding the deck's natural affinity-fueled deployment, while Prophetic Prism smooths your mana and cantrips on entry to keep the engine rolling.
tips: Flood artifacts to discount spells
Protect biggest artifact for Padeem draws
Grow Patchwork Automaton with artifact casts
Chain Thoughtcast for cheap draws
Stun blockers with Gilded Scuttler

decks.description: A mono-blue artifacts deck built around steady value rather than an explosive combo, using Padeem, Consul of Innovation to both protect its artifacts with hexproof and draw a card each upkeep as long as it controls the biggest artifact, alongside Experimental Aviator's twin Thopter tokens and Chief of the Foundry's team-wide pump. Treasure Keeper offers a free spell when it dies, Guardian Idol doubles as a mana rock or a 2/2 body in a pinch, and bounce spells like Blink of an Eye and Bury in Books buy time or reset problematic threats while Gilded Scuttler taps down and stuns an opposing blocker on entry, letting this fairly straightforward artifact value pile grind out card advantage and incremental board pressure.
tips: Keep biggest artifact for Padeem draws
Use Guardian Idol as flex mana/body
Stun key threats with Gilded Scuttler
Bounce blockers to reset combat
Cash in Treasure Keeper on death

decks.description: A black-green (with black-heavy mana) deathtouch tribal deck where every creature threatens to trade up in combat, and Hooded Blightfang converts that deathtouch synergy into direct damage: whenever any deathtouch creature you control attacks, each opponent loses 1 life and you gain 1, turning simple attacks into a life-total race. Aphelia, Viper Whisperer generates 1/1 deathtouch Snake tokens on attack for {1}{B/G}, and her activated ability lets any combat damage from your Gorgons and Snakes cut a defending player's life in half, a devastating finisher once the board is stocked with deathtouch bodies. Removal like Serpent Assassin, Vraska's Finisher, and Killing Glare clears the way for your deathtouch creatures to keep connecting, while Gorgon Flail and Coat with Venom retrofit any creature with deathtouch to enable Hooded Blightfang's drain trigger.
tips: Attack wide with deathtouch bodies
Trigger Blightfang drain every combat
Pump Aphelia's Snake tokens on attack
Halve life totals with Aphelia's ability
Equip Gorgon Flail for extra deathtouch

decks.description: This mono-black life-loss synergy deck turns its own self-damage into card advantage, headlined by Vilis, Broker of Blood, which draws a card for every point of life you lose from any source, and Dark Confidant, Read the Bones, and Metalspinner's Puzzleknot all pay life for cards that Vilis then converts into even more cards. Vampire Scrivener quietly grows a permanent +1/+1 counter whenever you gain or lose life on your turn, so the deck's own painful card draw and lifelink triggers from Scourge of the Undercity and Alchemist's Gift double as a way to build a real threat. Howling Banshee and Arrogant Outlaw punish the opponent directly with life loss, and once Vilis is on board even Infernal Grasp, Ulcerate, and Read the Bones' life payments cash in as extra cards, making every painful spell a two-for-one.
tips: Land Vilis to convert pain into cards
Pay life freely once Vilis resolves
Grow Vampire Scrivener via life swings
Lifelink triggers feed Scrivener too
Race with Howling Banshee/Arrogant Outlaw drains

decks.description: This deck is a black sacrifice/Treasure engine that wants to turn creatures and artifacts into mana rocks and card advantage while chipping away at the opponent's life total. Pitiless Plunderer and Dire Fleet Hoarder passively spit out Treasures whenever your creatures die, feeding Ruthless Knave and Evereth, Viceroy of Plunder, who both want sacrifice fodder to keep working (Evereth grows with +1/+1 counters and gains lifelink specifically off sacrificed Treasures). Deadly Dispute and Ruthless Knave's Treasure-sac ability turn all those spare artifacts into cards, while Reassembling Skeleton and Fake Your Own Death give you renewable bodies to feed the machine over and over. Ruthless Technomancer doubles as both a sac outlet that makes a pile of Treasures off a big creature and a reanimator that buys back that same creature later. Bastion of Remembrance punishes opponents every time a creature of yours dies, converting your death triggers into direct damage and life gain, and Shambling Ghast's death trigger can either shrink a blocker or add to the Treasure pile. The overall plan is a grindy, incremental value engine: sacrifice for value, refill your board and hand, and let small drains and Evereth's death trigger close out the game.
tips: Chain sacrifices for Treasures
Sac Treasures to Evereth for lifelink
Use Deadly Dispute to refill hand
Reanimate big bodies with Ruthless Technomancer
Let Bastion drain opponents on every death

decks.description: Like its sibling build, this deck leans on a core sacrifice-to-Treasure engine anchored by Pitiless Plunderer and Dire Fleet Hoarder, which convert creature deaths into free mana rocks, and Ruthless Knave and Deadly Dispute, which convert those Treasures into cards. Evereth, Viceroy of Plunder again serves as the payoff, growing with +1/+1 counters from any sacrifice and gaining lifelink specifically when a Treasure is the sacrificed permanent, then threatening a burst of damage when it dies. This version swaps in more direct removal-for-value: Contract Killing and Powerstone Fracture both destroy a creature while either making Treasures or requiring you feed the sacrifice engine, and Dusk Mangler lets you sacrifice a creature as an additional cost to blow out an opponent's hand, board, and life total simultaneously. Undercity Scrounger further rewards a death-heavy turn by tapping for a free Treasure once something has already died. The overall game plan is the same grindy attrition loop as Treasures 1 — sacrifice creatures, generate Treasures, refill cards, and win through incremental drain and a growing Evereth — just with slightly heavier removal to keep the opponent's board clear while you build your engine.
tips: Feed the sac engine constantly
Sac Treasures to Evereth for lifelink
Use Contract Killing for tempo and mana
Time Dusk Mangler for max blowout
Draw cards off spare Treasures

decks.description: This is a mono-black sacrifice-and-swarm deck built around turning creature deaths into insect tokens and incidental drain. Fumulus, the Infestation is the centerpiece: every time any player sacrifices a nontoken creature it spits out a flying 1/1 Insect, and whenever an Insect, Leech, Slug, or Worm you control attacks you drain the defending player for 1 life. Nantuko Husk and Devouring Swarm are built-in sac outlets that pump themselves by eating your other creatures, while Wriggling Grub actually gets better when it dies, replacing itself with two Worm tokens that also trigger Fumulus's drain ability when they attack. Village Rites turns any sacrifice into two cards, and Swarm of Bloodflies passively grows every time any creature (yours or an opponent's) dies, making it a steadily escalating threat in a deck full of death triggers. Removal like Go for the Throat, Eaten Alive, and kicked Heartstabber Mosquito clears blockers so your growing horde of small drain-triggering bodies can keep connecting.
tips: Sac fodder into Nantuko Husk/Devouring Swarm
Attack with Insects/Worms to drain
Village Rites for card draw
Let Wriggling Grub die on purpose
Kick Heartstabber Mosquito for removal

decks.description: This build again centers on Fumulus, the Infestation, converting any nontoken creature sacrifice into a flying Insect token and draining life whenever your Insects, Leeches, Slugs, or Worms attack. It leans harder into a repeatable sacrifice loop: Endless Cockroaches simply returns to your hand every time it dies, giving Nantuko Husk and Morkrut Necropod a nearly infinite sac outlet to pump the Husk or push through Morkrut Necropod's forced-sacrifice-on-attack cost. Wriggling Grub again turns one death into two Worm bodies that feed Fumulus's drain trigger, and Devouring Swarm gives you another flying sac outlet. This version adds more disruption than the first, with Virus Beetle and Torment of Scarabs stripping the opponent's hand and forcing repeated sacrifices or discards, alongside Go for the Throat and Eaten Alive as hard removal. The plan is a grindy, repeatable value loop: sacrifice Endless Cockroaches for value, keep growing your board of small drain-triggering insects, and squeeze the opponent's resources with discard while chipping away at their life total.
tips: Loop Endless Cockroaches into Husk
Attack with Insects/Worms to drain
Use Torment to force sacrifices

Sac fodder before combat for Fumulus tokens

decks.description: This deck is a black lifegain/reanimator pile that turns creature deaths and life gain into snowballing value. Taborax, Hope's Demise grows a +1/+1 counter every time another nontoken creature you control dies, draws a card off Cleric deaths specifically, and eventually flips on lifelink once it has five or more counters. Scion of the Swarm and Skemfar Shadowsage both reward the lifegain theme, with Scion putting a counter on itself whenever you gain any life, feeding off cards like Agonizing Syphon, Koma's Faithful, and the kicked Nullpriest of Oblivion's lifelink swings. The deck's reanimation package — Nullpriest of Oblivion's kicker, Bonecaller Cleric's sacrifice ability, Eerie Soultender's mill-then-return ability, and Revoke Demise — lets you mill or discard fatties like Archfiend's Vessel into the graveyard and bring them back, and Archfiend's Vessel specifically rewards being cast or returned from the graveyard by exiling itself to leave behind a 5/5 flying Demon token. Removal like Disfigure keeps the board clear while these recursive threats grind out an advantage through repeated life swings and value creatures returning again and again.
tips: Mill Archfiend's Vessel then reanimate it
Trigger Scion of the Swarm with lifegain
Sac Clerics to grow Taborax and draw
Use Eerie Soultender to refill graveyard value
Kick Nullpriest for a free reanimation

decks.description: This build again uses Taborax, Hope's Demise as its centerpiece, gaining +1/+1 counters whenever a nontoken creature you control dies and drawing extra cards specifically off Cleric deaths, eventually turning on lifelink once it's stacked five or more counters. Scion of the Swarm continues the lifegain-into-counters theme, growing off every life-gain trigger from cards like Koma's Faithful and Archfiend's Vessel's lifelink damage. The recursion package is built around Bonecaller Cleric's sacrifice-to-reanimate ability, Entomber Exarch's graveyard return or hand disruption, and Revoke Demise's discounted reanimation, letting you loop value creatures back from the graveyard repeatedly — and Archfiend's Vessel again rewards being cast or returned from the graveyard by exiling itself into a 5/5 flying Demon token. Elderfang Disciple and Entomber Exarch add a light discard/disruption angle to strip problem cards from the opponent's hand while your engine grinds forward. Priest of Gix ramps you into your bigger reanimation spells a turn early, and Severed Strands offers a two-for-one sacrifice-based removal spell that also gains life to keep Scion of the Swarm and Vito-style lifegain payoffs (via Taborax's lifelink) ticking upward.
tips: Reanimate Archfiend's Vessel for a 5/5 Demon
Grow Taborax off Cleric deaths
Ramp with Priest of Gix
Disrupt hands with Entomber/Elderfang
Severed Strands for removal plus lifegain

decks.description: This deck is built around a specific tribal payoff: Dark Supplicant can sacrifice three Clerics to tutor Scion of Darkness straight out of your deck, hand, or graveyard onto the battlefield for free, turning a critical mass of small Cleric creatures into a trampling threat that steals creatures from an opponent's graveyard whenever it connects. Around that payoff, Vito, Thorn of the Dusk Rose converts every point of life you gain into direct opponent life loss, making cards like Koma's Faithful's lifelink, Agonizing Syphon's lifegain-burn, and Moment of Craving's lifegain removal into de facto extra reach. Bonecaller Cleric and Revoke Demise provide additional reanimation to rebuy Clerics or bigger threats from the graveyard, while Eerie Soultender mills and later retrieves creatures to fuel the graveyard-based plans. Skemfar Shadowsage's mode that drains life off your greatest shared creature type synergizes naturally with a Cleric-heavy board. The overall plan is to assemble Clerics, either grinding with lifegain-drain synergies via Vito or going over the top by cheating Scion of Darkness into play with Dark Supplicant.
tips: Sac 3 Clerics for Scion of Darkness
Stack lifegain with Vito in play
Attack with Scion to steal graveyard creatures
Reanimate with Bonecaller/Revoke Demise
Count Clerics for Shadowsage's drain

cards.title: Vito, Thorn of the Dusk Rose

decks.description: This version again features Vito, Thorn of the Dusk Rose turning any life gained into life lost for an opponent, making every lifelink hit from Archfiend's Vessel or lifegain spell from Alchemist's Gift and Extinguish the Light double as extra reach. Priest of Forgotten Gods is a powerful engine piece here, sacrificing two other creatures to simultaneously drain opponents, force them to sacrifice a creature of their own, ramp black mana, and draw a card — an efficient value/removal/ramp combo all in one activation. Bonecaller Cleric provides graveyard recursion to rebuy whatever you fed to Priest of Forgotten Gods or lost to combat, and Priest of Gix helps ramp you into these black-heavy costs a turn ahead of schedule. Scion of the Swarm again converts any lifegain trigger into a growing flying threat, while Stronghold Confessor rewards a slower kicker line with a beefy menace body. Extinguish the Light and Revoke Demise round out the removal and recursion suite. The overall game plan is to use Priest of Forgotten Gods as a repeatable sacrifice-drain-ramp engine while Vito converts all your incidental lifegain into a parallel life-loss clock on the opponent.
tips: Sac fodder to Priest of Forgotten Gods
Stack lifegain triggers with Vito out
Recur creatures with Bonecaller Cleric
Ramp early with Priest of Gix
Kick Stronghold Confessor when able

cards.title: Vito, Thorn of the Dusk Rose

decks.description: This mono-black Vampire deck is built around lifegain triggers translating into card advantage and growing threats. Nazar, the Velvet Fang accumulates a feeding counter every time you gain life from any source, then lets you cash in three counters to draw three cards when it attacks, so cards like Skymarch Bloodletter's ETB drain, Blood Burglar and Scourge of the Undercity's lifelink, and Vampire of the Dire Moon's lifelink all directly fuel Nazar's card-draw engine. Sangromancer passively gains you life whenever an opponent's creature dies or they discard a card, which combos naturally with Bloodtithe Collector's discard-on-loss-of-life trigger and Gluttonous Guest's Blood tokens, which also gain you 1 life when sacrificed to loot away excess cards. Urge to Feed doubles as removal and a way to pump your whole Vampire board with +1/+1 counters, while Bleed Dry and Gift of Fangs handle bigger threats or punish non-Vampires on the battlefield. The deck's plan is to stack small, repeatable lifegain triggers to fuel Nazar's draw engine and grind out card advantage while Vampires with lifelink keep the life totals in your favor.
tips: Stack lifegain to fuel Nazar's draw
Loot with Blood tokens for value
Combine Sangromancer with discard effects
Urge to Feed doubles as pump and removal
Keep lifelink bodies attacking

decks.description: This build again leans on Nazar, the Velvet Fang's feeding-counter engine, turning any lifegain into a delayed card-draw payoff, fed here by Sanctum Seeker's attack-trigger drain, Scourge of the Undercity and Vampire of the Dire Moon's lifelink, and Creeping Bloodsucker's repeatable upkeep drain. Crossway Troublemakers is the key combat payoff, giving all attacking Vampires deathtouch and lifelink simultaneously, which both clears blockers efficiently and floods the board with lifegain triggers to power Nazar and Bloodthirsty Aerialist, the latter growing a +1/+1 counter every time you gain life. Crossway Troublemakers also lets you pay 2 life to draw a card whenever a Vampire dies, adding another layer of card advantage to the sacrifice fallout from combat. Gluttonous Guest's Blood token similarly nets a small life gain when sacrificed for a loot effect, and Urge to Feed again works as both removal and a team-wide Vampire pump. The overall game plan is an aggressive Vampire tribal swarm that turns combat damage into lifegain, lifegain into counters and card draw, and keeps refueling itself as Vampires trade in combat.
tips: Attack wide with Crossway online
Feed lifegain into Nazar's draw
Pay life off Vampire deaths to draw
Grow Bloodthirsty Aerialist via lifegain
Use Urge to Feed as pump/removal

decks.description: This deck is a mono-black lifegain-aggro pile built around vampires that punish opponents every time you gain life. Skymarch Bloodletter, Creeping Bloodsucker, and Vicious Conquistador all chip away at the opponent's life total on ETB or attack, while Scourge of the Undercity spreads lifelink around the team so multiple attackers trigger these effects each combat. The standout synergy is Nazar, the Velvet Fang: every point of lifegain from Creeping Bloodsucker, Scourge of the Undercity, or Gluttonous Guest's Blood token sacrifice piles feeding counters onto Nazar, letting you eventually strip three counters to draw three cards when it attacks. Necropolis Regent turns all that combat damage into permanent +1/+1 counters, snowballing your board while Thirsting Bloodlord pumps the whole vampire tribe. Removal like Bleed Dry and Go for the Throat clears blockers so your evasive threats keep connecting and keep the lifegain engine running.
tips: Feed Nazar with lifegain triggers
Spread lifelink via Scourge of the Undercity
Attack wide to grow Necropolis Regent
Use removal to protect attackers
Sacrifice Blood tokens for extra life triggers

decks.description: A lifegain-matters vampire deck where nearly every creature either gains life or benefits from it. Blood Burglar and the deathtouch-lifelink pair Vampire Nighthawk and Vampire of the Dire Moon trade profitably in combat while padding your life total, and Epicure of Blood converts every point of lifegain into direct life loss for the opponent, turning even small triggers like Moment of Craving's +2 life into extra damage. Nazar, the Velvet Fang again accumulates feeding counters from all this lifegain to eventually draw three cards, and Rodolf Duskbringer both protects itself with lifegain-triggered indestructible and can reanimate creatures at end of turn based on how much life you gained that turn. Exsanguinate acts as a scalable finisher, draining and gaining simultaneously to close out games once the board stalls.
tips: Chain lifegain into Epicure of Blood drains
Build Nazar's feeding counters early
Use Rodolf's end step reanimation off big lifegain turns
Trade profitably with deathtouch lifelinkers
Save Exsanguinate as a finisher

decks.description: This mono-black self-mill deck fills its own graveyard with cards like Gorging Vulture, Wailing Ghoul, and Grave Strength's mill-three effect, then leverages that graveyard for both value and payoff. Graveblade Marauder turns your graveyard's creature count into direct damage on combat hits, and Grave Strength converts that same pile into a pile of +1/+1 counters on a single creature. Gorex, the Tombshell is the deck's centerpiece combo: you can exile creatures from your graveyard to discount its cost dramatically, and later Gorex returns one of those exiled cards to hand at random whenever it attacks or dies, effectively refunding your investment. Gravedigger, Eternal Taskmaster, and Revoke Demise all recur creatures from the graveyard back to hand or battlefield, letting you rebuy your best threats repeatedly while removal like Murder and Deal Gone Bad keeps the board clear.
tips: Mill early to fuel Graveblade Marauder and Grave Strength
Exile creatures to discount Gorex
Recur threats with Gravedigger and Eternal Taskmaster
Attack with Gorex to get cards back
Time Revoke Demise for cheap early reanimation

decks.description: A self-mill sacrifice/reanimator deck that fills the graveyard quickly via Gorging Vulture and Undead Butler, then turns those cards into recurring value. Doomed Necromancer sacrifices itself to bring back any creature from the graveyard, and Undead Butler can be exiled on death to fetch a creature card from the graveyard back to hand, creating a steady stream of card advantage. Gorex, the Tombshell again lets you exile graveyard creatures to reduce its cost, then randomly returns one to hand when it attacks or dies. Moodmark Painter and Necrotic Wound both scale off your graveyard's creature count (Undergrowth), so the more you mill, the bigger the pump or removal effect becomes. Dusk Mangler and Fake Your Own Death add disruption and protection, letting your creatures dodge removal or force awkward sacrifices from the opponent.
tips: Mill aggressively to power Undergrowth cards
Sac Doomed Necromancer to reanimate a big threat
Exile graveyard creatures to cheat out Gorex
Use Fake Your Own Death to blank removal
Undead Butler dies into card advantage

decks.description: This deck mills itself with Gorging Vulture, Dreadhound, and Wailing Ghoul to build a graveyard, then repeatedly reanimates or rebuys creatures from it. Isareth the Awakener is the centerpiece: by paying X on attack you can return a creature card with mana value X directly to the battlefield with a corpse counter, essentially cheating threats into play for free combat value, though those creatures get exiled instead of dying again. Persistent Specimen offers a cheap, repeatable recursive body, while Dreadhound punishes both creature deaths and self-mill by draining the opponent whenever cards hit any graveyard. Gravedigger and Revoke Demise provide more traditional recursion, and Grave Strength again scales a creature's size directly off the number of creature cards you've milled into your yard.
tips: Mill early to enable Isareth's attack trigger

decks.description: A graveyard-recursion deck anchored by Liliana, Death's Majesty and Isareth the Awakener, both of which turn a stocked graveyard into repeated reanimation value. Liliana's +1 mills two cards while making a Zombie token, directly feeding the graveyard that her -3 then exploits to reanimate a creature as a Zombie; Isareth does similar work on attack by paying X to bring back a creature of that mana value. Goremand forces a sacrifice from the opponent when it enters, at the cost of you sacrificing a creature yourself, which pairs well with recursive bodies like Persistent Specimen or Undead Butler that don't mind dying since they can come back. Gorging Vulture and Undead Butler feed the graveyard early, while Gravedigger and Revoke Demise provide additional ways to rebuy threats, letting the deck grind out opponents through repeated resource attrition.
tips: Mill with Liliana and Vulture to enable reanimation
Sac expendable creatures to Goremand
Use Isareth attacks to cheat threats into play
Rebuy value creatures with Gravedigger
Protect Liliana to ultimate for a board wipe

decks.description: This is a mono-black sacrifice/aristocrats deck where creature deaths generate repeated value and life drain. Falkenrath Noble drains a point of life every time any creature dies, and Morbid Opportunist draws a card the first time creatures die each turn, so cheap death triggers from Doomed Dissenter (leaves a 2/2 Zombie) and Ozox, the Clattering King (leaves a Jumblebones token that can even return Ozox to hand) feed both engines simultaneously. Village Rites turns a sacrifice into two cards, and Tragic Slip becomes a devastating -13/-13 removal spell once a creature has already died that turn (Morbid), which is easy to trigger given how many creatures want to die anyway. Harvester of Souls further rewards this death-heavy plan by drawing a card whenever another nontoken creature dies, and Moan of the Unhallowed with flashback keeps refilling the board with Zombie tokens to sacrifice again.
tips: Chain deaths into Falkenrath Noble drains
Trigger Morbid before casting Tragic Slip
Sac tokens to Village Rites for cards
Let Ozox die to recur itself via Jumblebones
Use Doomed Dissenter as free sac fodder

decks.description: A mono-black aristocrats deck that squeezes value out of every creature death. Gnawing Zombie is a repeatable sacrifice outlet that drains a point of life on demand, feeding both Falkenrath Noble's death trigger and setting up Morbid conditions for Tragic Slip, which becomes -13/-13 removal once a creature has died that turn. Doomed Dissenter and Ozox, the Clattering King both leave bodies behind when they die, giving you cheap, repeatable fodder to sacrifice into Village Rites for two cards or Gnawing Zombie's drain. Bloodgift Demon adds a card-draw engine (at a life cost) on top of the sacrifice shell, while Morbid Opportunist turns the first death of a turn into a card every turn. Moan of the Unhallowed's flashback and Blood Beckoning's kicker help refill both the graveyard-recursion and token plans late game.
tips: Use Gnawing Zombie to trigger Morbid on demand
Sac fodder into Village Rites for cards
Let Ozox and Doomed Dissenter die profitably

decks.description: This mono-black sacrifice deck rewards creature deaths with card draw, life drain, and removal. Falkenrath Noble and Morbid Opportunist both trigger off any creature dying, and Deadly Dispute turns a sacrificed creature or artifact into two cards plus a Treasure token, smoothing mana while feeding the graveyard. Reaper from the Abyss is the deck's payoff bomb: once a creature has died in a turn (Morbid), it destroys a non-Demon creature at each end step, effectively acting as a recurring removal spell as long as something keeps dying. Tragic Slip again scales into -13/-13 removal off the same Morbid condition, and Wriggling Grub replaces itself with two 1/1 tokens on death, giving you more fodder to keep the sacrifice engine and Morbid triggers running turn after turn. Ozox, the Clattering King and its Jumblebones token add another self-sustaining death loop to the mix.

Let Reaper from the Abyss snipe threats each turn
