decks.description: A mono-black aristocrats deck built to grind value from repeated deaths and then close games with a massive Zombie swarm. Falkenrath Noble and Morbid Opportunist punish and reward every creature death, while Shambling Ghast and Doomed Dissenter leave behind removal effects or Zombie tokens when they die, keeping the sacrifice engine fed. Deadly Dispute converts any of that fodder into two cards and a Treasure, smoothing the mana for the deck's expensive top end. That top end is Army of the Damned, which creates thirteen 2/2 Zombie tokens outright and can be recast from the graveyard via flashback for an enormous second wave; Crawl from the Cellar's flashback then lets you recur a creature and buff one of those Zombies with a +1/+1 counter, tying the recursion package directly into the Zombie go-wide plan. Ozox, the Clattering King and its Jumblebones token add yet another recurring death trigger to keep Falkenrath Noble and Morbid Opportunist firing.

decks.description: This deck is built around the two-Brothers-Yamazaki gimmick and other creatures that punish opponents for not having enough blockers or that hit hard once buffed. Because there are exactly two permanents named Brothers Yamazaki in the deck, having both on the battlefield turns off the legend rule for them and each grants the other +2/+2 and haste, turning two mediocre 1/1 bushido bodies into a pair of 3/3 hasty attackers the turn they land together. Mogg Flunkies wants company to attack or block, which the deck naturally provides with token makers like Krenko's Command and Goblin Surprise, while Battle-Rattle Shaman and Weaselback Redcap push through extra damage on key swings. Fiendish Duo's damage-doubling ability turns any burn spell like Furious Reprisal or Pigment Storm, or even combat damage, into a blowout finisher. The overall plan is a low-curve mono-red beatdown deck that goes wide with cheap creatures and tokens, then closes games with pump spells and direct damage once Fiendish Duo is in play to double up the final points of damage.

Pair Mogg Flunkies with tokens
Fiendish Duo doubles burn spells
Use Goblin Surprise for width or pump
Curve out fast, swing early

decks.description: This is a mono-red 'pinger' deck that wants to chip away at the opponent's life total with small triggered damage effects and then amplify that damage into a finishing blow. Firebrand Archer and Firespitter Whelp both ping opponents for 1 whenever you cast a noncreature (or Dragon) spell, while Goblin Fireslinger provides a repeatable tap-for-damage effect. The key payoff is Wildfire Elemental and Chandra's Spitfire, which both trigger off noncombat damage dealt to an opponent: Wildfire Elemental pumps your whole team +1/+0 and Chandra's Spitfire itself grows +3/+0, so a single ping from Firebrand Archer or a burn spell like Play with Fire can suddenly make your whole board hit much harder that turn. Rionya, Fire Dancer adds a late-game combo angle, copying another creature you control at combat equal to 1 plus instants/sorceries cast that turn, letting you flood the board with hasty temporary copies right as your spells trigger all your damage-based payoffs.

Chain pings into Wildfire Elemental/Spitfire
Copy creatures with Rionya after spells
Use burn to close, not just clear

decks.description: This deck is a mono-red Dragon tribal build anchored by Lathliss, Dragon Queen, whose ability to spawn a 5/5 flying Dragon token every time another nontoken Dragon enters can snowball a small number of Dragons into a huge army very quickly. Dragonlord's Servant discounts your Dragon spells to help you deploy them faster, while Carnelian Orb of Dragonkind ramps mana and grants haste to Dragons cast off it. Rapacious Dragon and Seize the Spoils generate Treasure tokens for extra acceleration and card advantage, and burn spells like Dragon's Fire and Bathe in Dragonfire clear blockers or finish off opponents, with Dragon's Fire scaling its damage to a Dragon's power when you reveal or choose one. The overall plan is to ramp into and chain Dragon after Dragon, using Lathliss's token generation to turn each additional Dragon into two threats instead of one, then close out with flying damage and direct burn.

Use Treasures to accelerate
Dragon's Fire scales off Dragon power
Chain Dragons for token snowball

decks.description: Like its companion deck, this is a mono-red Dragon tribal deck centered on Lathliss, Dragon Queen turning every subsequent nontoken Dragon into a bonus 5/5 flier, but here it's paired with Sarkhan, Fireblood as a second card-advantage and ramp engine that can also ultimate into four more 5/5 flying Dragon tokens if the game goes long. Sarkhan's +1 to add mana restricted to Dragon spells combos directly with Dragonlord's Servant's cost reduction to help cast Dragons ahead of curve, and Hellkite Whelp adds reach by pinging a blocker whenever it attacks. Sarkhan's Rage is a big finisher that deals 5 damage to any target, with a small downside if you control no Dragons, tying its best use to the deck's Dragon-heavy game plan. The deck wants to plant Lathliss or use Sarkhan's mana to deploy Dragons quickly, converting each subsequent Dragon into extra board presence, then finish with direct damage from Sarkhan's Rage or Dragon's Fire.

Lathliss snowballs extra Dragons

decks.description: This is a spells-matter deck built around Zada, Hedron Grinder: any instant or sorcery that targets only Zada gets copied for every other creature you control, so pump spells like Invigorated Rampage or Crimson Wisps become team-wide buffs off a single card. The deck backs this up with a suite of spell-triggered creatures — Guttersnipe pings opponents for 2 per spell, Kiln Fiend and Spellgorger Weird both grow off spells cast, and Young Pyromancer makes a 1/1 Elemental token with every instant or sorcery — turning a small burst of cheap spells into a wide board and a big damage swing. Scholar of Combustion and Cyclops Electromancer reward filling the graveyard with spells, letting you rebuy key instants/sorceries or blow up a blocker based on graveyard count. The overall plan is to jam cheap cantrip-style pump spells (Fists of Flame, Renegade Tactics, Fire Prophecy) into Zada for exponential value, while Young Pyromancer and Guttersnipe passively add bodies and damage along the way.

decks.description: Another Zada, Hedron Grinder spells deck, this build leans even harder into recurring instants and sorceries to keep the Zada engine running longer. Charmbreaker Devils returns a random instant or sorcery from your graveyard each upkeep and grows +4/+0 whenever you cast one, while Scholar of Combustion lets you flash back a specific spell from the yard, and two copies of Ancestral Anger reward filling the graveyard by getting bigger (+1/+0 more) for each copy already there. As with the companion build, any spell that targets only Zada copies onto every other creature you control, so cheap all-upside cantrips like Hearts on Fire or Uncaged Fury become team-wide double strike or pump effects, while Young Pyromancer and Firespitter Whelp add incidental tokens and damage. The deck's game plan is to grind through its spell-based value engines (Charmbreaker Devils, Scholar of Combustion, Ancestral Anger) to keep refueling Zada triggers turn after turn.

Recur spells with Charmbreaker Devils

decks.description: This is a mono-red Warriors tribal aggro deck built around the Coward mechanic: Gornog, the Red Reaper, Boldwyr Intimidator, and Pyrophobia all turn opposing creatures into Cowards, which then simply can't block your Warriors. Gornog additionally pumps all your attacking Warriors by +X/+0 equal to the number of Cowards your opponents control, so the more blockers you strip away, the bigger your attack gets — a direct synergy between the Coward-making effects and Gornog's attack trigger. Kruin Striker grows and gains trample whenever another creature enters, rewarding a wide curve of cheap Warriors, while Expedition Champion gets bigger simply by having another Warrior around. Relic Axe further rewards Warrior tribal synergy by giving +2/+1 instead of +1/+1 when equipped to a Warrior. The plan is to flood the board with Warriors, strip away blockers via the Coward mechanic, and swing with an ever-growing, mostly unblockable attack force.
tips: Turn blockers into Cowards
Gornog scales with Cowards made
Kruin Striker rewards wide boards
Relic Axe favors Warriors

decks.description: Like its companion deck, this mono-red Warriors build revolves around making opposing creatures into Cowards so they physically cannot block your Warriors, using Gornog, the Red Reaper, Kargan Intimidator, Boldwyr Intimidator, and Pyrophobia to strip blockers away. Gornog then rewards this plan directly, pumping all attacking Warriors by +X/+0 where X equals the number of Cowards your opponents control, so every Coward you create is both a removed blocker and added power. Kargan Intimidator is a flexible one-card toolbox that can pump itself, make Cowards, or grant trample, while Unstoppable Ogre and Daybreak Combatants add tempo by pushing damage through or buffing a creature the turn they enter. The overall game plan is aggressive: deploy Warriors, strip blockers with Coward effects, and close games quickly with buffed, largely unblockable attackers backed by burn like Soul Sear.

decks.description: This is a red heroic/spellslinger hybrid deck that wants to target its own creatures with cheap combat tricks to trigger repeated value. Satyr Hoplite and Cleon, Merry Champion both have Heroic, gaining a +1/+1 counter or card advantage respectively whenever a spell targets them, while Arena Athlete's Heroic trigger shuts down a blocker, so a single pump spell aimed at these creatures does double duty as both a buff and a value engine. Frontline Heroism supercharges this plan directly: it creates a 1/1 haste Soldier token and then, whenever you cast a spell targeting only a single creature you control, it creates another token and copies that spell onto the new token, effectively doubling every single-target trick you cast. Twinferno gives you a built-in copy effect or double strike option, and Kiln Fiend/Scholar of Combustion add more spell-triggered payoffs and graveyard recursion. The deck's plan is to jam single-target pump spells onto its Heroic creatures and let Frontline Heroism multiply the value into an army of extra Soldier tokens and copied effects.
tips: Target Heroic creatures with tricks
Frontline Heroism doubles single-target spells
Arena Athlete removes blockers via targeting
Recur spells with Scholar of Combustion

decks.description: This heroic-themed red deck leans on repeatedly targeting a small core of creatures with cheap pump spells to trigger value, and adds Dreadhorde Arcanist as a graveyard engine that lets you recast a cheap instant or sorcery for free whenever it attacks (as long as its mana value is at or below the Arcanist's power). Satyr Hoplite grows with a +1/+1 counter each time it's targeted, Cleon, Merry Champion exiles and lets you play the top card of your library on being targeted, and Arena Athlete and Hero of the Games both turn single-target spells into tempo or team-wide pump when they're the target, meaning a single trick like Titan's Strength or Coordinated Assault can snowball multiple bonuses across your board. Unleash Fury doubling a creature's power is a strong finisher, especially on an already-pumped Heroic creature or Kiln Fiend after several spells. The overall plan is to build a small, resilient board of Heroic threats, keep re-targeting them with cheap tricks to trigger their abilities, and use Dreadhorde Arcanist to keep recurring your best cheap spells for extra value each combat.

Dreadhorde Arcanist recasts cheap spells

decks.description: This deck is a heroic/spells-matter aggro pile built around cheap creatures that reward you for targeting them with your own spells. Cleon, Merry Champion, Akroan Crusader, and Arena Athlete all have Heroic triggers, so even a single combat trick like Titan's Strength or Hearts on Fire generates extra value — Cleon impulse-draws a card, Akroan Crusader spawns a hasty Soldier token, and Arena Athlete locks down a blocker. Young Pyromancer converts any instant/sorcery cast into a 1/1 Elemental, stacking bodies as you fire off pump spells, and Frontline Heroism doubles down by creating a token and then copying any single-creature-targeting spell onto it, effectively giving you two pump spells for one. Mirrorwing Dragon is the payoff bomb: if you target only it with an instant/sorcery, you copy that spell onto every other creature you control, turning something like Unleash Fury or Twinferno into a team-wide blowout. Scholar of Combustion adds recursion, letting you recast a spent trick like Roil Eruption. The overall plan is to flood the board cheaply, then unload combat tricks that both pump the team and trigger a wall of heroic/token abilities to close out the game quickly.

decks.description: Similar to its sibling Stoked deck, this is a heroic-and-spells aggro deck that wants to cast a steady stream of cheap instants/sorceries into a board of creatures that reward being targeted. Cleon, Merry Champion and Akroan Crusader turn any targeted trick into card advantage or an extra hasty body, while Hero of the Games pumps the whole team whenever it's targeted, and Weaver of Lightning pings an opposing creature on every instant/sorcery cast, doubling as reach and removal. Young Pyromancer again converts spellcasting into a stream of 1/1 Elemental tokens to go wide. Goblin Dark-Dwellers is the recursive payoff, letting you re-cast a cheap spell like Infuriate or Roil Eruption for free straight from the graveyard, effectively getting two uses out of your best trick. The gameplan is to keep casting cheap pump/burn spells that target your creatures to chain heroic triggers, grow an army of tokens, and finish with direct damage from Roil Eruption or a lethal alpha strike buffed by Uncaged Fury's double strike.

decks.description: A classic go-wide Goblin tribal aggro deck that floods the board with tokens and then buffs them all at once. Krenko's Command and Goblin Surprise each add bodies, while General Kreat, the Boltbringer punishes wide boards by dealing direct damage to opponents every time another creature enters, meaning every token you make is also a small burn spell. Dropkick Bomber is the anthem, giving all other Goblins +1/+1, and its activated ability can turn a token into a one-shot flying kamikaze attacker. Battle Cry Goblin grants haste and +1/+0 to the team and can trigger its own Pack Tactics for another attacking token if your total attacking power is high enough. Volley Veteran scales its removal with your Goblin count, so it's best played after you've already built a board. The plan is simple: flood the board with cheap tokens and Goblins, pump wide with Goblin Surprise or Weaselback Redcap, and swing for lethal while Kreat chips in extra damage from every new arrival.

General Kreat pings on every ETB

decks.description: This Goblin tribal deck again centers on flooding the board with 1/1 tokens and turning that width into damage. Krenko's Command, Goblin Surprise, and Goblin Goliath (which makes tokens equal to your opponent count) all add cheap bodies, and General Kreat, the Boltbringer converts each of those entering creatures into a point of direct damage to the opponent. Dropkick Bomber anthems the tribe and can convert a token into a one-time evasive attacker via its sacrifice-on-damage flying ability. Goblin Goliath doubles as a finisher, letting you pay to double damage from a source once it's online, which combos well with a big Volley Veteran ping or a buffed attacker. Mogg Flunkies wants company to attack or block, which the wide token board naturally provides. Battle Cry Goblin gives the team haste and +1/+0, letting freshly made tokens swing immediately, while Goblin Arsonist offers a small parting shot when it dies. The deck wants to curve out with cheap Goblins and tokens, then overwhelm with combat tricks and Kreat's incidental burn.

Goblin Goliath doubles damage late game
Attack wide so Mogg Flunkies can swing
Battle Cry Goblin gives haste to new tokens

decks.description: This Goblin deck is built to snowball quickly around Krenko, Tin Street Kingpin: every attack grows Krenko with a +1/+1 counter and then creates that many 1/1 Goblin tokens, so an unanswered Krenko can flood the board in just a couple of turns. General Kreat, the Boltbringer converts every one of those newly created tokens into a point of direct damage to the opponent, meaning a big Krenko swing doubles as a burn spell. Goblin Smuggler makes a Goblin unblockable to push through damage (ideal for protecting or enabling Krenko's attack), while Beetleback Chief and Krenko's Command add more instant bodies. Volley Veteran's damage scales with your Goblin count, so it hits hardest after Krenko has gone off. Scavenged Blade adds a cheap equip buff to keep pressure going. The overall plan is to protect and attack with Krenko every turn to generate an exponentially growing army, using Kreat's damage trigger and evasion effects to close out the game fast.
tips: Protect Krenko at all costs
Attack with Krenko every turn to snowball

Use Goblin Smuggler to push Krenko through

decks.description: This Goblin tribal deck leans on Goblin Rabblemaster as its engine: it forces other Goblins to attack each turn, spawns a hasty 1/1 Goblin token at the start of every combat, and grows its own power based on how many other Goblins attack alongside it, quickly generating an unstoppable wide board. General Kreat, the Boltbringer again turns every entering creature — including Rabblemaster's tokens — into a direct damage trigger against the opponent. Fanatical Firebrand and Destructive Digger provide small utility, letting you convert a spent creature or extra land/artifact into either a burn ping or a card. Battle Cry Goblin buffs and hastes the team, letting Rabblemaster's freshly made tokens swing the turn they arrive. Fissure Wizard and Fire Prophecy smooth draws and answer opposing blockers. The deck's plan is to jam Rabblemaster early, let its forced attacks and token generation snowball the board width, and ride Kreat's incidental burn plus removal spells to close the game out.
tips: Land Goblin Rabblemaster early
Let Rabblemaster force through attacks
General Kreat burns on every new token
Battle Cry Goblin gives new tokens haste

decks.description: This is a discard/Blood token aggro deck that turns hand disruption into card advantage and growing threats. Anje's Ravager forces you to discard your hand and draw three cards every time it attacks, feeding Ivora, Insatiable Heir, which gets a +1/+1 counter every time you discard a card — so Anje's Ravager attacking can single-handedly grow Ivora by three counters in one swing. Blood tokens generated by Belligerent Guest, Falkenrath Celebrants, Voldaren Epicure, and Blood Petal Celebrant all let you loot away excess or dead cards (discard a card, draw a card), which both fuels Ivora's counters and enables the Madness cards like Incorrigible Youths and Abandon Reason to be cast cheaply straight from discard. Voldaren Duelist and Incorrigible Youths add evasive haste damage, while Flame-Blessed Bolt and Soul Sear provide efficient removal. The deck wants to attack aggressively, discard aggressively to trigger Madness and grow Ivora, and use the constant stream of Blood tokens to keep refilling the hand with only the cards it wants.
tips: Attack with Anje's Ravager to loot big
Discard cards into Ivora for counters
Crack Blood tokens to trigger Madness
Use Voldaren Duelist to push damage through
Save removal for key blockers

decks.description: This Vampire-tribal deck blends aggressive combat damage triggers with a Blood token subtheme for card selection and removal. Markov Enforcer fights an opposing creature every time it or another Vampire enters, and if that fight kills something, it creates a Blood token — pairing well with Ivora, Insatiable Heir and Markov Blademaster, both of which grow via combat damage or discarding. Blood Hypnotist rewards you for cracking Blood tokens by making a creature unable to block, directly enabling your attackers (like double-striking Markov Blademaster) to connect. Insolent Neonate and the various Blood tokens (from Blood Petal Celebrant, Falkenrath Celebrants) give you discard-and-draw fuel to dig for removal or grow Ivora off discards. Markov Retribution doubles as a team pump and a Vampire-powered removal spell, letting your biggest Vampire fight down a blocker. The plan is to curve Vampires that trigger Markov Enforcer's fight ability, keep cracking Blood tokens to shut off blockers via Blood Hypnotist, and ride Markov Blademaster's snowballing counters to close out games.
tips: Chain Vampire ETBs into Markov Enforcer fights

Use Markov Retribution as removal or pump

decks.description: This Vampire deck focuses on unblockable-style aggression backed by a Treasure/Blood value engine. Markov Warlord and Voldaren Duelist both make blockers step aside when they enter, clearing the way for your team — including Ivora, Insatiable Heir and Belligerent Guest, both of which create Blood tokens whenever they connect with a player, which in turn feed Ivora's discard-triggered counters. Scion of Opulence converts dying nontoken Vampires into Treasure tokens, and it can sacrifice two artifacts (Treasures or Blood tokens) to impulse-draw a free card, tying together the deck's sacrifice fodder into card advantage. Insolent Neonate offers a cheap discard-and-sacrifice outlet for extra digging. Blood Feud and Vampiric Fury double as removal or a combat trick, letting your Vampires fight or swing through with first strike and +2/+0. The overall gameplan is to push through unblockable damage with the Duelist/Warlord effects, grow Ivora off discards, and use Scion of Opulence's artifact sacrifice to keep drawing extra cards.
tips: Use Warlord/Duelist to clear blockers
Sac two artifacts with Scion for a free card

decks.description: This Vampire aggro deck is built around explosive combat damage triggers that snowball quickly. Falkenrath Marauders is the standout payoff: it has flying and haste, and puts two +1/+1 counters on itself every time it connects with a player, so it can grow from a 3/3 into a huge threat within a couple of unblocked attacks. Ivora, Insatiable Heir and Belligerent Guest both generate Blood tokens on connecting damage, which you crack via discard to grow Ivora further and to smooth draws. Voldaren Ambusher scales its damage with your Vampire count, making it a strong removal piece once your board is established, while Voldaren Duelist clears the way for an attacker (ideal for protecting Falkenrath Marauders' swing). Neonate's Rush is a cheap, card-advantage-positive burn spell that gets cheaper with any Vampire in play. Soul Sear and Blood Feud round out the removal suite for anything that would otherwise block your fliers or ground threats. The plan is to get Falkenrath Marauders connecting as early and often as possible while using Blood tokens and cheap removal to keep the damage train rolling.
tips: Protect Falkenrath Marauders once it connects
Use Voldaren Duelist to clear its path
Crack Blood tokens for Ivora counters

Neonate's Rush is nearly free value

decks.description: This mono-red deck is built entirely around the exert mechanic, turning what looks like a drawback into a snowballing value engine. Nearly every creature can exert as it attacks to generate an extra effect — Ahn-Crop Crasher stops a blocker, Emberhorn Minotaur pumps itself and gains menace, Nef-Crop Entangler grows bigger, and Anep, Vizier of Hazoret exiles and lets you play the top two cards of your library. Battlefield Scavenger ties the whole theme together: whenever ANY creature you control exerts, you get to loot a card, smoothing draws every time you commit to the aggressive attack. Bloodlust Inciter offsets the exert downside by handing haste to a creature that got tapped down, effectively letting you double-dip on attackers turn after turn. Cartouche of Zeal and Trial of Zeal add reach and removal, with the neat trick that any Cartouche returns Trial of Zeal to hand for a repeatable 3-damage burn spell. Fling gives a finishing burst by sacrificing a big exerted attacker straight at the opponent's face.
tips: Exert every attacker for value
Battlefield Scavenger loots off any exert
Bloodlust Inciter refreshes tapped attackers

decks.description: A leaner version of the exert-aggro shell, this deck again centers on Battlefield Scavenger's loot trigger off any exerted creature, paired with Ahn-Crop Crasher and Sandstorm Crasher for tempo-positive exert triggers. Sandstorm Crasher is a standout here: exerting it creates a tapped, attacking copy of another creature you control, effectively doubling up an attacker's damage for the turn without needing a full extra combat. Anep, Vizier of Hazoret continues to exile-and-play cards off its exert trigger, while Bloodlust Inciter grants haste to refresh a creature that exerted last turn. Pathmaker Initiate and Cartouche of Zeal keep small creatures unblockable, and Zealot of the God-Pharaoh provides a late-game mana sink that pings the opponent directly. Trial of Zeal is again recurred by Cartouche of Zeal for repeatable direct damage, and Fling turns any big attacker into a reach spell.

decks.description: This build leans hardest into extra-combat shenanigans thanks to Combat Celebrant, which when exerted untaps all your other creatures and grants an additional combat phase — a genuine payoff for a deck full of creatures that want to attack twice. Trueheart Twins amplifies this further: whenever ANY creature you control exerts (including Combat Celebrant itself), all your creatures get +1/+0, meaning a double-combat turn triggered by Combat Celebrant also pumps the whole team via Trueheart Twins. Battlefield Scavenger still offers the loot-on-exert value seen in the other Zealots decks, while Anep, Vizier of Hazoret and Nef-Crop Entangler add their own exert upside. Hearts on Fire and Fling provide combat tricks and reach, and Trial of Zeal recurs off Cartouche of Zeal for repeatable burn. The overall plan is to build a board, then use Combat Celebrant to effectively double your attack step while Trueheart Twins keeps buffing every exert along the way.

decks.description: This variant blends the exert theme with a hard tempo push using Zealous Conscripts, which steals an opponent's permanent with haste for a turn — great for stealing a blocker to swing with or borrowing a creature to Fling. Sandstorm Crasher's exert creates a tapped, attacking copy of another creature, and Battlefield Scavenger again loots off any exert trigger in the deck. Trueheart Twins buffs the whole team every time a creature exerts, stacking with Nef-Crop Entangler and Anep, Vizier of Hazoret's own exert bonuses. Bloodlust Inciter grants haste to refresh an exerted attacker, and Minotaur Sureshot's mana sink helps push through extra damage late. Cartouche of Zeal and Trial of Zeal again form the deck's removal-and-recursion package, while Fling turns a stolen or pumped creature into direct damage to close out the game.

decks.description: This green Food-tribal deck generates a steady stream of Food tokens from ETB effects — Fierce Witchstalker, Orchard Strider, Tough Cookie, and Trail of Crumbs all create Food the moment they hit the battlefield — and then converts that Food into card advantage, life gain, and combat power. Trail of Crumbs is the key engine piece: whenever you sacrifice a Food, you can pay {1} to dig two cards deep for a permanent, meaning every Food you crack (for life, for Maraleaf Rider's forced-block ability, or for Giant Opportunity/Insatiable Appetite) also refills your hand. Hurska Sweet-Tooth turns any life gain, including from cracking Food, into a temporary +X/+X pump equal to the life gained, so sacrificing Foods for 3 life each can suddenly swing combat math. Giant Opportunity offers a build-around choice — sac two Foods for a 7/7 Giant or just make three more Foods to keep the engine running — while Tireless Provisioner ensures you never run dry on Food or Treasure off your land drops.

decks.description: This green deck builds around the 'modified' keyword — any creature with a +1/+1 counter, Aura, or Equipment counts — to unlock trample and mana bonuses across the board. Kodama of the West Tree gives every modified creature trample and fetches a basic land tapped whenever one connects with a player, turning combat damage into ramp. Gnarlid Colony independently grants trample to any creature with a +1/+1 counter, stacking redundantly with Kodama's effect. Orochi Merge-Keeper is a clean payoff: once it becomes modified (even by a single +1/+1 counter from Snakeskin Veil or Defend the Celestus), it taps for double green mana, accelerating the rest of the deck. Heir of the Ancient Fang enters bigger if you already control a modified creature, rewarding you for committing counters or auras early. Wolfrider's Saddle creates its own 2/2 Wolf and immediately attaches, giving you a modified creature straight out of the gate, while Audacity and Warbriar Blessing add combat tricks that replace themselves (Audacity draws a card when it leaves play) or remove a blocker via fight.

decks.description: A go-wide Saproling tribal deck where token generation and anthem effects compound quickly. Spore Swarm and Saproling Migration flood the board with 1/1 tokens, Tukatongue Thallid and Spore Crawler leave value behind when they die, and Sporecrown Thallid pumps every other Fungus or Saproling by +1/+1, making even a modest token swarm hit much harder. Shroofus Sproutsire is the deck's key payoff: whenever a Saproling deals combat damage to a player, it creates that many additional Saproling tokens, so an unblocked wide attack snowballs into an even bigger board for the next turn. Might of the Masses and Overwhelm scale directly with your creature count, turning a wide board into a one-shot kill via a single pumped attacker or a team-wide alpha strike. Fungal Plots offers a late-game sacrifice outlet, turning excess Saprolings into life and cards.

Sporecrown Thallid buffs Fungi and Saprolings, and Shroofus Sproutsire again converts unblocked Saproling damage directly into more Saproling tokens, creating a compounding snowball. Verdeloth the Ancient is the standout scaling payoff here: kicked for X, it creates X Saproling tokens on entry and then permanently pumps all your Saprolings and Treefolk by +1/+1, effectively turning a wide token board into a much bigger one instantly. For the Family and Overcome both scale their payoff based on board width (four-plus creatures triggers a bigger buff, or a team-wide +2/+2 trample swing), giving this deck strong alpha-strike finishers once the token count is high enough.

decks.description: A green ramp-into-Beasts deck that uses +1/+1 counters as its core payoff. Slinza, the Spiked Stampede discounts Beast spells and gives every other Beast an extra +1/+1 counter on entry, while also offering a repeatable removal option: whenever Slinza or another creature with power 4+ enters, paying {1}{R/G} lets Slinza fight an opposing creature, effectively turning big creature drops into removal spells. Advocate of the Beast and Hungry Megasloth continue growing creatures with counters turn after turn, and Gnarlid Colony grants trample to anything with a +1/+1 counter, meaning the deck's counter-laden Beasts naturally punch through blockers. Woodland Liege converts each Beast entering the battlefield into a card, keeping the deck's gas flowing, while Explore and Gift of the Gargantuan smooth out land drops and refill the hand with more threats. Predator's Strike and Time to Feed round out the removal and finishing package, letting a big counter-laden creature fight or push through for lethal.

decks.description: This Beast-counters deck is anchored by Garruk, Caller of Beasts, whose +1 refills your hand with every creature revealed off the top five cards, and whose -3 can cheat a green creature straight onto the battlefield, bypassing mana costs entirely on a big Beast. Slinza, the Spiked Stampede again discounts Beasts and hands out bonus +1/+1 counters, while its fight ability (triggered whenever a power-4+ creature enters, including a cheated-in threat from Garruk) doubles as removal. Garruk's Packleader draws a card whenever another power-3+ creature enters, so ramping into big Beasts via Garruk's -3 or Baloth Gorger's kicker also refills the hand. Gnarlid Colony's trample-for-counters ability lets these counter-laden threats punch through, and Bushwhack/Time to Feed provide flexible fights or land fixing. The overall plan is to accelerate into oversized Beasts, using Garruk and Garruk's Packleader to keep the cards flowing while Slinza's fight trigger clears the way.

decks.description: This is a mono-green ramp-into-huge-creatures deck built around cheating out or discounting massive Dinosaurs. Ghalta, Primal Hunger costs {X} less based on total power of creatures you control, so ramp spells like Rampant Growth and mana dorks such as Drover of the Mighty (which itself becomes a 4/4 threat as long as you control a Dinosaur) help both cast Ghalta cheaply and pump its cost reduction. Thundering Spineback anchors the tribal synergy by giving all other Dinosaurs +1/+1 and can also manufacture extra 3/3 trampling Dinosaur tokens in the late game, while Savage Stomp becomes a cheap two-for-one removal/pump spell whenever it targets one of your Dinosaurs. Cherished Hatchling is the standout synergy piece here: when it dies, all Dinosaur spells you cast that turn gain flash and an enters-the-battlefield fight trigger, letting you ambush an opponent's creature by flashing in something like Colossal Dreadmaw or Thundering Spineback and immediately fighting down a blocker or attacker. Predator's Strike and Tail Swipe give this build reach in combat, turning a Dinosaur into a temporary trampling bruiser or forcing an unfavorable fight, while Thundering Spineback again lords over the tribe with a global +1/+1 and a late-game token mill.

Biogenic Upgrade compounds this further by both placing counters across up to three creatures and then doubling them, and Duskshell Crawler grants trample to every countered creature so all that accumulated power actually gets through combat damage.

Oran-Rief Ooze puts a counter on all attacking creatures that already have a counter whenever it attacks, so once a few creatures have counters, every attack step compounds their size, and Mowu, Loyal Companion again converts any single counter addition into +1 extra, becoming the biggest beneficiary of Invigorating Surge (which places a counter then doubles the total) or Jiang Yanggu's -1 ability. Mowu, Loyal Companion again scales absurdly fast whenever it receives counters (getting one extra each time), and Biogenic Upgrade's double-then-distribute effect combined with Servant of the Scale's death trigger (which dumps its counters onto another creature) lets you relocate a big pile of counters onto your best remaining threat if it dies. Armorcraft Judge turns a wide, countered board into a burst of card draw by drawing one card per creature with a counter, and Quirion Beastcaller grows every time you cast a creature spell while its death trigger spreads its accumulated counters across your board, meshing well with sacrifice-adjacent value. decks.description: This deck is built around aggressive land ramp that converts extra lands directly into card advantage and bigger threats. Braulios of Pheres Band has power and toughness equal to your land count and draws a card plus lets you play an extra land whenever it attacks, meaning every attack both grows your board and refuels your hand, especially when paired with lots of fetch effects like Ainok Guide, Shardless Outlander's landcycling, and Go Forth's land tutoring. Somberwald Beastmaster dumps three tokens onto the battlefield at once and gives all your creature tokens deathtouch, making even the smallest Wolf or Elf token a dangerous attacker or blocker, while Woodland Champion turns every token entering (including Ambassador Oak's Elf Warrior and all three Beastmaster tokens) into a +1/+1 counter on itself, quickly ballooning into a real threat. Jadelight Ranger explores twice on entry, giving it the best chance to hit lands for your hand while stacking +1/+1 counters when it whiffs, and it's joined by Ixalli's Diviner, Merfolk Branchwalker, and the plain Explore spell itself, all doing the same land-or-counter selection to keep the deck consistently hitting land drops without diluting quality. Braulios of Pheres Band again scales with total lands and refuels your hand every attack, while Primeval Herald search for a basic land on both entry and attack, meaning the deck's land count climbs quickly to power up Braulios and keep the explore triggers finding action rather than lands late.

decks.description: This deck is a straightforward mono-green Elf tribal pile that leans on lord effects and combat tricks to push through damage. Leaf-Crowned Visionary and Dionus, Elvish Archdruid both pump and empower your Elf army, while Llanowar Elves ramps you into your bigger threats like Razorgrass Invoker's late-game +3/+3 pump ability. The deck's core synergy is Dionus, Elvish Archdruid making all your Elves untap and grow a +1/+1 counter whenever they become tapped on your turn — meaning any effect that taps your Elves outside of attacking (like Leaf-Crowned Visionary's card draw or general utility) keeps them combat-ready. Overcome and Band Together serve as finishers, either pumping the whole team with trample or having creatures fight down blockers, and Bounty of Skemfar smooths draws by finding both lands and Elves. Overall this is a curve-out tribal aggro deck that wants to flood the board early and close with an alpha strike buffed by Overcome or Arbor Armament's counter.

Hunter's Edge doubles as both a pump spell and cheap removal by having a buffed creature fight an opponent's blocker. Dionus, Elvish Archdruid again lets tapped Elves (including Marwyn after she's tapped for mana) untap and gain a permanent +1/+1 counter, directly reinforcing Marwyn's own counter-based growth and letting her generate even more mana next time. Dwynen's Elite creates extra token bodies to feed Marwyn's trigger, while Llanowar Visionary and Llanowar Elves provide early ramp to get Marwyn and other Elves online faster. Band Together and Overcome give the deck reach, turning a wide, buffed board into lethal damage in a single turn. The overall plan is to flood the board with cheap Elves to grow Marwyn into a massive mana source, then dump that mana into big spells or simply overrun with pumped creatures.
tips: Play Elves to grow Marwyn
Untap Marwyn via Dionus
Ramp hard, then overrun
Dwynen's Elite feeds counters
Finish with Overcome or Band Together

decks.description: This is a black-green Elf deck built around Tyvar Kell, whose static ability lets all your Elves tap for black mana, opening up splashes and enabling his loyalty abilities that pump, untap, and eventually create an Elf-tribal card advantage emblem. Voice of the Woods rewards you for building a wide Elf board by letting you tap five Elves to create a massive 7/7 trample Elemental, which combines naturally with Dionus, Elvish Archdruid's ability to untap tapped Elves and grow them with a counter — you can tap Elves into Voice of the Woods' cost, then have Dionus untap some of them for blocking or further activations the same turn. Ivy Lane Denizen adds extra counters whenever any green creature enters, working well alongside token generators like Tyvar's 0 ability. Reckless Amplimancer and Hunter's Edge give the deck reach and interaction, doubling a creature's stats or turning a pumped creature into removal. Overall this deck wants to go wide with cheap Elves, use Tyvar Kell to fix mana and grow the team, and eventually convert its board into a giant trampling threat via Voice of the Woods.
tips: Tyvar fixes Elf mana to black
Tap 5 Elves for 7/7 trampler
Dionus untaps key Elves
Ivy Lane Denizen stacks counters
0-ability builds board fast

decks.description: This mono-green landfall deck wants to hit extra land drops per turn to trigger a suite of creatures and equipment that scale off it. Lotus Cobra generates a burst of mana on every land drop, which directly fuels Harrow (sacrifice a land, search two basics onto the battlefield) into double landfall triggers in a single spell, and feeds bigger plays like Undergrowth Champion's damage-preventing counters or Baloth Woodcrasher's massive +4/+4 trample pump. Kazandu Stomper actively bounces up to two of your own lands back to hand specifically so you can replay them later for extra landfall triggers, working alongside Sutina, Speaker of the Tajuru's attack trigger that also bounces a land to grow a creature with a counter. Skyclave Pick-Axe turns any landfall trigger into an equipped creature buff, and Bite Down provides clean removal off your biggest threat's power. The overall plan is to jam extra lands each turn — through bounce and search effects — to repeatedly trigger landfall payoffs and grow an overwhelming board.
tips: Replay bounced lands for value
Harrow doubles landfall in one turn
Kazandu Stomper resets land drops
Lotus Cobra ramps off land drops
Bite Down as removal finisher

decks.description: This green landfall deck is built to double and recur its land-drop triggers rather than just play more lands. Ancient Greenwarden is the centerpiece: it lets you replay lands from your graveyard and causes any landfall trigger on your permanents to happen twice, turning every one of your other landfall creatures — Canopy Baloth, Snapping Gnarlid, Territorial Scythecat, Kazandu Nectarpot, and Retreat to Kazandu — into double-value engines the moment it's on the battlefield. Kazandu Stomper's enters-the-battlefield ability to bounce up to two of your lands feeds right back into Ancient Greenwarden's graveyard-replay and doubling, letting you generate several landfall triggers from a single creature. Sutina, Speaker of the Tajuru adds another land-bounce-and-counter engine on attack, while Krosan Tusker and Bond of Flourishing smooth your draws by cycling into lands or finding permanents. Bite Down again offers efficient removal scaled to your biggest creature. This deck's identity is grinding out incremental value from repeated, doubled landfall triggers rather than a single explosive turn.
tips: Ancient Greenwarden doubles landfall
Replay lands from graveyard
Kazandu Stomper bounces for more triggers
Territorial Scythecat snowballs counters
Cycle Krosan Tusker for land

decks.description: This landfall deck focuses on stacking multiple land drops in a single turn to maximize counter-based payoffs. Scythecat Cub is the standout card here: its landfall ability puts a +1/+1 counter on a creature, but if it's the second landfall trigger that turn, it doubles the number of counters on that creature instead — meaning cards like Harrow (which sacrifices a land but searches two more basics into play) or Kazandu Stomper's land bounce set up a second land drop the same turn to explode Scythecat Cub's counter output. Murasa Ranger offers an optional payment to double up counters as well, while Territorial Scythecat and Snapping Gnarlid provide steady incremental growth from every land drop. Sutina, Speaker of the Tajuru's attack-triggered land bounce combines with these landfall payoffs to generate extra triggers even after your main phase plays are done. Cliffhaven Kitesail gives evasion to push through a big attacker once it's sufficiently pumped. The overall plan is to chain multiple land drops each turn to snowball counters onto a couple of key threats.
tips: Chain two land drops for double counters
Harrow sets up second landfall
Kazandu Stomper bounces then replays
Sutina bounces lands on attack
Equip Kitesail for evasive threat

decks.description: This landfall deck is built to maximize the value of every land drop through both extra land-fetching spells and a wide array of landfall payoffs. Harrow sacrifices a land to search two basics directly onto the battlefield, generating two landfall triggers from a single card that immediately power up Baloth Woodcrasher's huge +4/+4 trample buff, Groundswell's doubled +4/+4 pump (since a land entered that turn), and token generation from Sporemound. Sutina, Speaker of the Tajuru's attack trigger bounces a land to grant a +1/+1 counter, letting you replay that land later for another landfall trigger, while Scythecat Cub doubles its own counter output if it's the second landfall trigger to resolve in a turn — something Harrow's double land drop enables directly. Skyclave Pick-Axe and Grazing Gladehart round out the payoffs with combat buffs and incidental lifegain. This deck wants to jam extra land drops via Harrow and Sutina to trigger as many landfall abilities as possible in a single turn, then swing with hugely pumped trampling creatures.
tips: Harrow doubles land drops
Groundswell hits harder same turn
Scythecat Cub rewards second trigger
Sutina bounces then replays land
Swing wide with Woodcrasher

decks.description: This five-color Jund-leaning deck is built entirely around the Cascade mechanic, casting spells that dig into the library for free extra value. Bloodbraid Elf, Enlisted Wurm, Maelstrom Colossus, Bituminous Blast, and Violent Outburst all cascade into a free spell on resolution, and Averna, the Chaos Bloom sweetens every one of those cascades by letting you also drop a land from among the exiled cards straight onto the battlefield tapped, effectively ramping and fixing mana every time you cascade. The deck supports its greedy mana base with Chromatic Lantern (making all your lands tap for any color) and a suite of basic-landcycling spells (Shardless Outlander, Fiery Fall, Ash Barrens) to smooth out color screw before you start chaining cascades. Bituminous Blast and Fiery Fall double as removal alongside their cascade or landcycling utility, giving the deck some interaction while it digs for free spells. The overall plan is simply to jam as many cascade spells as possible, using Averna and Chromatic Lantern to keep mana consistent, and let the random extra value from cascading snowball into a winning board.
tips: Chain cascades for free spells
Averna nets a free land too
Chromatic Lantern fixes colors
Landcycle early to hit drops
Use Bituminous Blast as removal
